
#####  🏥 Hospital of collection {.tabset}

```{r table_hospital, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(rstatix)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Merge hospital variables
# -----------------------------

hospital_df <- BISC %>%
  select(
    id_bisc,
    bl_hosp.col_m_12w,
    bl_hosp.recr_m_32w,
    bl_hosp_m_0y
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(-id_bisc, ~ as.numeric(as.character(.))))
biomarkers_center_df <- biomarkers_df %>%
  left_join(
    hospital_df,
    by = c("Participant_ID" = "id_bisc")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Hospital_raw = case_when(
      Visit == "v1" ~ bl_hosp.col_m_12w,
      Visit == "v2" ~ bl_hosp.recr_m_32w,
      Visit == "v3" ~ bl_hosp_m_0y,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

# -----------------------------
# 2. Recode hospitals
# -----------------------------
biomarkers_center_df <- biomarkers_center_df %>%
  mutate(
    Hospital = case_when(
      Hospital_raw == 10 ~ "Sant Pau",
      Hospital_raw == 11 ~ "Maternitat",
      Hospital_raw == 12 ~ "Sant Joan",
      TRUE ~ "Missing"
    ),
    Hospital = factor(Hospital,
                      levels = c("Sant Pau", "Maternitat", "Sant Joan", "Missing"))
  )
biomarkers_center_df <- biomarkers_center_df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Result_numeric),
    !is.na(Hospital_raw)
  )
biomarkers_center_df <- biomarkers_center_df %>%
  filter(Hospital_raw != "Missing")

# -----------------------------
# 3. Clean biomarker values
# -----------------------------
biomarkers_center_df <- biomarkers_center_df %>%
  mutate(
    Result_numeric = as.character(Result_numeric),
    Result_numeric = gsub("[^0-9,\\.]", "", Result_numeric),
    Result_numeric = gsub(",", ".", Result_numeric),
    Result_numeric = as.numeric(Result_numeric)
  )
biomarkers_center_df <- biomarkers_center_df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Result_numeric),
    !is.na(Hospital)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 4. Define colors
# -----------------------------
hospital_colors <- c(
  "Sant Pau" = "#1f77b4",
  "Maternitat" = "#ff7f0e",
  "Sant Joan" = "#2ca02c"
#  "Missing" = "grey70"
)

# -----------------------------
# 5. Visualization (dots emphasized)
# -----------------------------
ggplot(biomarkers_center_df, 
       aes(x = Hospital, y = Result_numeric)) +
  
  geom_boxplot(
    aes(fill = Hospital),
    alpha = 0.2,
    outlier.shape = NA
  ) +
  
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = Hospital),
    width = 0.2,
    alpha = 0.7,
    size = 2
  ) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ Visit + Biomarker, scales = "free_y", drop = TRUE) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = hospital_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = hospital_colors) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Biomarker values by hospital (batch effect check)",
    x = "Hospital",
    y = "Biomarker value"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

# -----------------------------
# 6. Kruskal-Wallis test
# -----------------------------
hospital_kw <- biomarkers_center_df %>%
  group_by(Visit, Biomarker) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(Hospital) >= 2) %>%
  kruskal_test(Result_numeric ~ Hospital) %>%
  add_significance("p")

kable(hospital_kw, caption = "Kruskal-Wallis test by hospital")

# -----------------------------
# 7. Pairwise Wilcoxon tests
# -----------------------------
hospital_pairwise <- biomarkers_center_df %>%
  group_by(Visit, Biomarker) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(Hospital) >= 2) %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(
    Result_numeric ~ Hospital,
    p.adjust.method = "BH"
  ) %>%
  add_significance("p.adj")

kable(hospital_pairwise, caption = "Pairwise Wilcoxon (BH-adjusted)")

# -----------------------------
# 8. Summary table
# -----------------------------
hospital_summary <- biomarkers_center_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Result_numeric)) %>%
  group_by(Visit, Biomarker, Hospital) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median = median(Result_numeric, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = IQR(Result_numeric, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

kable(hospital_summary, caption = "Summary by hospital")
```


##### 🧫 Excluded samples {.tabset}

```{r table_excluded, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
########## EXCLUDED/REJECTED
# Filter rows that were excluded or rejected
excluded_df <- biomarkers_df %>%
  filter(Excluded == TRUE | Rejected == TRUE | is.na(Excluded) | is.na(Rejected))
# Columns to show
excluded_df_display <- excluded_df %>%
  select(Participant_ID, Biomarker, Visit, Result_numeric, ComOthr, Excluded, Rejected)


excluded_or_rejected <- biomarkers_df$Excluded | biomarkers_df$Rejected
excluded_df_display %>%
  kable("html", caption = "Excluded / Rejected biomarker results", digits = 2) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
missing_entries_result <- sum(is.na(biomarkers_df$Result_numeric))
missing_entries_ee <- sum(is.na(biomarkers_df$Excluded) | is.na(biomarkers_df$Rejected))
num_excluded_or_rejected <- sum(excluded_or_rejected, na.rm = TRUE)
num_rows <- nrow(biomarkers_df)
percent_excluded_or_rejected <- (num_excluded_or_rejected / num_rows) * 100

# Display counts nicely
cat("<strong>Total rows in the biomarker dataset:</strong>", num_rows, "<br>")
cat("<strong>Results Excluded or Rejected:</strong>", num_excluded_or_rejected, "<br>")
cat("<strong>Percentage of Excluded or Rejected:</strong>", round(percent_excluded_or_rejected,2), "%<br>")
cat("<strong>Number of missing entries in Excluded or Rejected:</strong>", missing_entries_ee, "<br>")
cat("<strong>Number of missing entries in biomarker results:</strong>", missing_entries_result, "<br>")

# Drop excluded/rejected rows
biomarkers_df <- biomarkers_df[!excluded_or_rejected, ]
num_final_rows <- nrow(biomarkers_df)
cat("<strong>Number of final rows after dropping Excluded/Rejected:</strong>", num_final_rows, "<br><br>")
```

#### 0.2.2. Counts and visits {.tabset}

**👥 PARTICIPANT COUNTS**

```{r table_0343234, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
######## Counts
# Count unique participants overall
total_unique_participants <- n_distinct(biomarkers_df$Participant_ID)
cat("<strong>Total unique participants with biomarkers available:</strong>", total_unique_participants, "<br><br>")
```

____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

📅 **BIOMARKER AVAILABILITY PER VISIT**

```{r table_0343234234234, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
######## Biomarker visit patterns
df <- biomarkers_df %>% filter(!is.na(Result_numeric))

visit_pattern_table <- function(data, biomarker_name){
  data %>%
    filter(Biomarker == biomarker_name) %>%
    group_by(Participant_ID) %>%
    summarise(visits = paste(sort(unique(Visit)), collapse = "+"),
              .groups = "drop") %>%
    count(visits, name = "n_participants") %>%
    arrange(desc(n_participants))  # order from highest to lowest
}

# Generate tables
tables <- list(
  pTau217 = visit_pattern_table(df, "pTau217"),
  NFL    = visit_pattern_table(df, "NFL"),
  GFAP   = visit_pattern_table(df, "GFAP")
)

# Print tables nicely
for(name in names(tables)){
  cat("<br><strong>Visit patterns for", name, ":</strong><br>")
  tables[[name]] %>%
    kable("html", caption = paste("Visit pattern table for", name)) %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover")) %>%
    print()
}

######## Biomarker summary per visit
# Create wide table: biomarkers as rows, visits as columns
biomarker_visit_counts <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Result_numeric)) %>%                # only filled results
  group_by(Biomarker, Visit) %>%
  summarise(n_participants = n_distinct(Participant_ID), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Visit,
    values_from = n_participants,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  arrange(Biomarker)

# Print in HTML
cat("<br><strong>Number of participants with data per biomarker per visit:</strong><br>")
biomarker_visit_counts %>%
  kable("html", caption = "Participants with filled biomarker results per visit") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"))
```

#### 0.2.3. Outliers {.tabset}

📈 **OUTLIERS**

Biomarker outliers will be detected through (a) stablished cut-offs, (b) Interquartile ranges (IQR) (±3sd) methods. Visual evaluation will also be considered to define technical and biological outliers.

```{r biomarker_plot_outliers, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}

######## Plot biomarker trajectories
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# Clean Result_numeric
biomarkers_df <- biomarkers_df %>%
  mutate(
    Result_clean = str_remove_all(Result_numeric, "pg/ml|\\s"),
    Result_clean = str_replace_all(Result_clean, "(?<=\\d),(?=\\d{1,3}$)", "."),
    Result_clean = ifelse(Result_clean %in% c("-999999990","-1000000","-10000","<LOD","NA","-"), NA, Result_clean),
    Result_numeric = as.numeric(Result_clean)
  )

# Filter valid values
df <- biomarkers_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Result_numeric) & !is.na(Biomarker) & !is.na(Visit))
df$Visit <- factor(df$Visit, levels = c("v1","v2","v3","v4"), ordered = TRUE)

# Plot trajectories
ggplot(df, aes(x = Visit, y = Result_numeric, group = Participant_ID, color = Participant_ID)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ Biomarker, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Participant biomarker trajectories across visits",
    x = "Visit",
    y = "Biomarker value (pg/ml)",
    color = "Participant"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

########### APPROACH 1
# Check outliers
thresholds <- c("NFL" = 50, "GFAP" = 200, "pTau217" = 1)

outliers <- biomarkers_df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Result_numeric) & 
      ((Biomarker == "NFL" & Result_numeric > thresholds["NFL"]) |
       (Biomarker == "GFAP" & Result_numeric > thresholds["GFAP"]) |
       (Biomarker == "pTau217" & Result_numeric > thresholds["pTau217"]) |
       Result_numeric < 0)
  ) %>%
  select(Participant_ID, Visit, Biomarker, Result_numeric, Result_clean) %>%
  arrange(Participant_ID, Biomarker, Visit)

# Build dynamic threshold text
threshold_text <- paste(paste(names(thresholds), thresholds, "pg/mL"), collapse=", ")
```
____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

1️⃣ **Threshold approach**

```{r outliergraph, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
# Print message
cat("<strong>The original value (Result_numeric) will be compared to the cleaned value (Result_clean). Thresholds:</strong> ", threshold_text, "<br><br>")

# Show outliers as HTML table
if(nrow(outliers) > 0){
  outliers %>%
    kable("html", caption = "Participant biomarker outliers", row.names = FALSE) %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
} else {
  cat("No outliers found.<br>")
}
```

____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

2️⃣ **IQR approach**

```{r iqrapproach, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
########### APPROACH 2
# Compute IQR thresholds per biomarker
iqr_thresholds <- df %>%
  group_by(Biomarker) %>%
  summarise(
    Q1 = quantile(Result_numeric, 0.25),
    Q3 = quantile(Result_numeric, 0.75),
    IQR = Q3 - Q1,
    Lower = Q1 - 3*IQR,
    Upper = Q3 + 3*IQR,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Identify outliers using IQR ±3
outliers_iqr <- df %>%
  left_join(iqr_thresholds, by = "Biomarker") %>%
  filter(Result_numeric < Lower | Result_numeric > Upper) %>%
  select(Participant_ID, Visit, Biomarker, Result_numeric, Result_clean, Lower, Upper) %>%
  arrange(Biomarker, Participant_ID, Visit)

# Print dynamic thresholds
cat("<strong>Outliers defined using IQR ±3 per biomarker:</strong><br>")
iqr_thresholds %>%
  mutate(Label = paste(Biomarker, ": [", round(Lower,2), "-", round(Upper,2), "]", sep="")) %>%
  pull(Label) %>%
  paste(collapse = ", ") %>%
  cat("<br><br>")

# Display outliers as HTML table
if(nrow(outliers_iqr) > 0){
  outliers_iqr %>%
    kable("html", caption = "Participant biomarker outliers (IQR ±3)", row.names = FALSE) %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"))
} else {
  cat("No outliers detected.<br>")
}
```


____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

**CONCLUSION**:

The IQR approach seems too exhaustive. Outliers detected by both methods appear to be biological, as visually corroborated, rather than procedural or methodological artifacts (same participant has generally high values). Therefore, they will not be removed from the database unless specified by the researchers.

#### 0.2.4. Normality {.tabset}

Biomarker datasets will be transformed in a wide format and normality will be assessed to confirm if further transformations (e.g., log) may be needed. Two strategies will be performed for normality calculation: (1) Shapiro Wilk test, (2) visual inspection with histogram and qqplots.

📊 **NORMALITY**

1️⃣ Normality test

```{r wideformat, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
############################# Biomarker in vertical format
biomarkers_wide <- df %>%
  select(Participant_ID, Visit, Biomarker, Result_numeric) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Biomarker,
    values_from = Result_numeric
  ) %>%
  arrange(Participant_ID, Visit)

# Check result
#head(biomarkers_wide)

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

biomarkers_long <- biomarkers_wide %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(Participant_ID, Visit),
    names_to = "Biomarker",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Value))

normality_results <- biomarkers_long %>%
  group_by(Biomarker, Visit) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    shapiro_p = shapiro.test(Value)$p.value,
    normality = ifelse(shapiro_p > 0.05,
                       "Data are consistent with a normal distribution",
                       "Data significantly deviate from a normal distribution"),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

normality_results %>%
  mutate(shapiro_p = round(shapiro_p,4)) %>%
  kable(
    "html",
    col.names = c("Biomarker","Visit","N","Shapiro p-value","Interpretation"),
    caption = "Shapiro-Wilk normality tests for each biomarker per visit"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE,
                bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"))

```

____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
2️⃣ Plots

```{r wideformatplots, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
###### Plots
library(ggplot2)

# Ensure factors
biomarkers_long$Biomarker <- factor(biomarkers_long$Biomarker)
biomarkers_long$Visit <- factor(biomarkers_long$Visit)

ggplot(biomarkers_long, aes(x = Value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(Biomarker ~ Visit, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of biomarkers by visit",
    x = "Biomarker value",
    y = "Count"
  )
```
```{r qqplot, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
ggplot(biomarkers_long, aes(sample = Value)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line() +
  facet_grid(Biomarker ~ Visit, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "QQ plots for normality assessment",
    x = "Theoretical quantiles",
    y = "Sample quantiles"
  )
```

____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

**CONCLUSION**:

Most biomarkers deviate from a normal distribution, often showing partial skewness. Log transformations may be applied for analyses requiring approximate normality. Residual normality may be assessed after fitting models as part of model diagnostics.

```{r, results='hide', message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# Calculation of log and z-scores
biomarkers_wide <- biomarkers_wide %>%
  mutate(
    log_pTau217 = log(pTau217),
    log_NFL    = log(NFL),
    log_GFAP   = log(GFAP)
  )

biomarkers_wide <- biomarkers_wide %>%
  group_by(Visit) %>%
  mutate(
    z_log_pTau217 = scale(log_pTau217)[,1],
    z_log_NFL    = scale(log_NFL)[,1],
    z_log_GFAP   = scale(log_GFAP)[,1]
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```

### 0.3. Transformed variables {.tabset}

Dataset will be changed so that each participant has one row per visit, and gestational age will be calculated for each participant (correcting an inconsistent gestational age in the process).

#### Comorbidity groups {.tabset}

It is included in another form, it should be added to the dataset.

```{r comorbidity groups, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

medical <- read.csv(
  "C:/Users/U127925/Desktop/FLH/Analyses/External/BISC - 05032026/databases/medical_background_iblocles.csv",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

vars_to_add <- c(
  "MUJERES_HIPERTENSAS","MUJERES_TROMBOSIS","MUJERES_TROMBO",
  "MUJERES_CARDIOISQUEMICA","MUJERES_CARDIOESTRUCTURAL_CUALI",
  "MUJERES_CARDIO_ESTRATIFICADA","OTRAS_CARDIOVASCULAR","MUJERES_ACV",
  "MUJERES_MIGRAÑA","MUJERES_EPILEPSIA","OTRAS_NEUROLOGICAS_LIMPIAR",
  "NEUROLOGICAS_ESTRATIFICADAS","SUENO_VIGILIA_LIMPIAR",
  "OTRAS_SUENO_VIGILIA","NEUROLOGICAS_SUENOVIGILIA_ESTRATIFICADAS",
  "SUENO_VIGILIA","CONJUNTO_NEUROLOGICAS_SUENOVIGILIA",
  "MUJERES_DISLIPEMIA","MUJERES_DIAB1","MUJERES_DIAB2",
  "ENDOCRINO_METABOLICA","ENDOCRINA","OTRAS_METABOLICA",
  "ENDOCRINAS_NOTIROIDES","CONJUNTO_NO_TIROIDEO","METABOLICAS_DIGESTIVAS",
  "METABOLICAS_NO_RCV","CONJUNTO_MBO_NO_RCV","MUJERES_TIROIDEA",
  "ENFERMEDAD_TIROIDEA_ESTRATIFICADA","OTRAS_TIROIDES","CONJUNTO_TIROIDEA",
  "MUJERES_DIGESTIVOS","DIGESTIVO_ESTRATIFICADO","DIGESTIVO_LIMPIAR",
  "OTROS_DIGESTIVOS","CONJUNTO_DIGESTIVO","MUJERES_DERMATITIS",
  "DERMATOLOGICAS_ESTRATIFICADO","OTRAS_DERMATOLOGICAS",
  "DERMATOLOGICA_AUTOINMUNE","CONJUNTO_DERMATOLOGICAS",
  "MUJERES_AUTOINMUNE","AUTOINMUNE_LIMPIA","AUTOINMUNE_ESTRATIFICADA",
  "CONJUNTO_AUTOINMUNE","MUJERES_ASMA","MUJERES_RINITIS",
  "RINITS_ESTRATIFICADA","OTRAS_RINITIS","HIPERSENSIBILDAD_RESPIRATORIA",
  "CONJUNTO_HIPERSENSIBILIDAD","MUJERES_URINARIA",
  "URO_RENAL_ESTRATIFICADAS","CONJUNTO_URORENAL",
  "INFECCIOSAS_ESTRATIFICADAS","OTRAS_INFECCIOSAS_ESTRATIFICADAS",
  "CONJUNTO_INFECCIOSAS","OTRAS_CONGENITAS","CONGENITAS_ESTRATIFICADAS",
  "CONJUNTO_CONGENITAS","MUJERES_RENALCRONICA","MUJERES_COAGULACION",
  "COAGULACION_ESTRATIFICADO","MUJERES_OTRAS_HEMATOLOGICAS",
  "OTRAS_HEMATOLOGICAS","TRASTORNO_COAGULACION","HEMATOLOGICAS_ESTRATIFICADO",
  "CONJUNTO_HEMATOLOGICAS","DEFECTOS_COAGULACION_ESTRATIFICADO",
  "MUJERES_TUMORES","TUMORES","OTROS_TUMORES","TIROIDEO",
  "TUMORES_ESTRATIFICADO","CONJUNTO_TUMORES","PSIQUIATRIA",
  "ANTECEDENTES_PSIQUIATRICOS","OTRAS_OSTEOARTICULARES",
  "OSTEOARTICULARES_LIMPIAR","CONJUNTO_OSTEOARTICULAR",
  "OTRAS_GINECOLOGICAS","GINECOLOGICAS_LIMPIAR",
  "CONJUNTO_GINECO_OBSTETRICO","OTRAS_VASCULARPERIFERICO",
  "VASCULARPERIFERICO_LIMPIAR","CONJUNTO_VASCULARPERIFERICO",
  "OTRAS_OFTALMOLOGICAS","OFTALMO_LIMPIAR","CONJUNTO_OFTALMOLOGICAS",
  "OTRAS_DOLOR","CONJUNTO_DOLOR","MUJERES_TRANSFUSION",
  "MUJERES_CIRUGIAS","CIRUGIAS_GINECO_OBSTETRICAS_NO_CESAREA",
  "CESAREAS","CIRUGIA_CARDIOVASCULAR","CIRUGIA_DE_VARICES",
  "CIRUGIA_CABEZA_CUELLO","CIRUGIAS_OFTALMOLOGICAS",
  "CIRUGIAS_OTORRINOLARINGOLOGICAS","CIRUGIA_MAXILOFACIAL",
  "CIRUGIAS_DIGESTIVO_PARED_ABDOMINAL","CIRUGIA_BARIATRICA",
  "CIRUGIA_RINONES_VIAS_URINARIAS","CIRUGIAS_PIEL_PARTES_BLANDAS",
  "CIRUGIAS_TRAUMATOLOGICAS","CIRUGIAS_POR_URGENCIAS",
  "CIRUGIAS_ESTETICAS","CORRECCIONES_CONGENITAS",
  "OTRAS_CIRUGIAS_NO_AGRUPABLES","RIESGO_CARDIOVASCULAR",
  "CONJUNTO_RCV","CARDIO_ESTRUCTURAL","CARDIO_ARRITMIAS",
  "ANEMIA","ENFERMEDAD_CEREBROVASCULAR","CEFALEA",
  "OTRAS_NEUROLOGICAS","CONGENITOS_MBO","INTOLERANCIA_LACTOSA",
  "HIPERPLASIA_SUPRARRENAL","TRASTORNOS_HIPOFISARIOS"
)

medical_sub <- medical %>%
  select(id_bisc, all_of(vars_to_add))

# QC before merging
cat("BISC participants:", n_distinct(BISC$id_bisc), "\n")
cat("Medical participants:", n_distinct(medical_sub$id_bisc), "\n")

cat("Overlap IDs:", sum(BISC$id_bisc %in% medical_sub$id_bisc), "\n")

BISC_before <- BISC
BISC <- BISC %>%
  mutate(id_bisc = as.character(id_bisc))

medical_sub <- medical_sub %>%
  mutate(id_bisc = as.character(id_bisc))

BISC_merged <- BISC %>%
  left_join(medical_sub, by = "id_bisc")

# ROW INTEGRITY
cat("Rows before:", nrow(BISC_before), "\n")
cat("Rows after:", nrow(BISC_merged), "\n")

# ADD COLS
new_cols <- setdiff(names(BISC_merged), names(BISC_before))

cat("New variables added:", length(new_cols), "\n")
print(new_cols)

common_cols <- intersect(names(BISC_before), names(medical_sub))

common_cols

missing_after <- sapply(new_cols, function(v) sum(is.na(BISC_merged[[v]])))

summary(missing_after)

stopifnot(
  nrow(BISC_before) == nrow(BISC_merged),
  all(BISC_before$id_bisc %in% BISC_merged$id_bisc)
)


# Take the merged df as the definitive one
BISC <- BISC_merged
```

#### APOE {.tabset}

It is included in another form in SNP form, it should be added to the dataset.

```{r apoe, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# =========================================================
# 1. LOAD APOE DATA
# =========================================================

apoe_df <- read.csv(
  "C:/Users/U127925/Desktop/FLH/Analyses/External/BISC - 05032026/databases/FilteredSNPs_APOE.csv",
  sep = ","
)

# =========================================================
# 2. FILTER SAMPLE
# Keep subjects with 7th digit of IID = 1
# =========================================================

apoe_df <- apoe_df %>%
  mutate(IID = as.character(IID)) %>%
  filter(
    nchar(IID) >= 7 &
    substr(IID, 7, 7) == "1"
  )

# =========================================================
# 3. STANDARDISE IDS
# =========================================================

apoe_df$id_bisc <- as.character(apoe_df$id_bisc)
BISC$id_bisc <- as.character(BISC$id_bisc)

# =========================================================
# 4. REMOVE DUPLICATES IN APOE
# =========================================================

apoe_df <- apoe_df %>%
  distinct(id_bisc, .keep_all = TRUE)

cat("APOE N after cleaning:", nrow(apoe_df), "\n")

# =========================================================
# 5. QC OVERLAP
# =========================================================

cat(
  "APOE not in BISC:",
  length(setdiff(apoe_df$id_bisc, BISC$id_bisc)),
  "\n"
)

cat(
  "BISC not in APOE:",
  length(setdiff(BISC$id_bisc, apoe_df$id_bisc)),
  "\n"
)

cat(
  "Common IDs:",
  length(intersect(apoe_df$id_bisc, BISC$id_bisc)),
  "\n"
)

# =========================================================
# 6. APOE GENOTYPE
# =========================================================
# IMPORTANT:
# Mapping depends on allele coding in the SNP file!
# =========================================================

apoe_df <- apoe_df %>%
  mutate(
    APOE_genotype = case_when(

      # e2/e2
      rs429358_T..C. == 2 &
        rs7412_C..T. == 0 ~ "e2/e2",

      # e2/e3
      rs429358_T..C. == 2 &
        rs7412_C..T. == 1 ~ "e2/e3",

      # e3/e3
      rs429358_T..C. == 2 &
        rs7412_C..T. == 2 ~ "e3/e3",

      # e2/e4
      rs429358_T..C. == 1 &
        rs7412_C..T. == 0 ~ "e2/e4",

      # e3/e4
      rs429358_T..C. == 1 &
        rs7412_C..T. == 1 ~ "e3/e4",

      # e4/e4
      rs429358_T..C. == 0 &
        rs7412_C..T. == 2 ~ "e4/e4",

      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# =========================================================
# 7. BUILD ε4 CARRIER VARIABLE
# =========================================================

apoe_df <- apoe_df %>%
  mutate(
    APOE_e4_carrier = case_when(

      APOE_genotype %in% c(
        "e2/e4",
        "e3/e4",
        "e4/e4"
      ) ~ 1,

      APOE_genotype %in% c(
        "e2/e2",
        "e2/e3",
        "e3/e3"
      ) ~ 0,

      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

# =========================================================
# 8. QC GENOTYPE DISTRIBUTION
# =========================================================

cat("\nAPOE genotype distribution:\n")

print(
  apoe_df %>%
    count(APOE_genotype) %>%
    arrange(desc(n))
)

cat("\nε4 carriers:\n")

print(
  table(
    apoe_df$APOE_e4_carrier,
    useNA = "ifany"
  )
)

# =========================================================
# 9. MERGE INTO BISC
# =========================================================

BISC <- BISC %>%
  left_join(
    apoe_df %>%
      select(
        id_bisc,
        APOE_genotype,
        APOE_e4_carrier
      ),
    by = "id_bisc"
  )

# =========================================================
# 10. FINAL QC
# =========================================================

cat("\nTotal BISC N:", nrow(BISC), "\n")

cat(
  "With APOE info:",
  sum(!is.na(BISC$APOE_e4_carrier)),
  "\n"
)

cat(
  "ε4 carriers:",
  sum(BISC$APOE_e4_carrier == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
  "\n"
)

# =========================================================
# 11. PLOT APOE GENOTYPE
# =========================================================

BISC %>%
  filter(!is.na(APOE_genotype)) %>%
  mutate(
    APOE_genotype = factor(
      APOE_genotype,
      levels = c(
        "e2/e2",
        "e2/e3",
        "e2/e4",
        "e3/e3",
        "e3/e4",
        "e4/e4"
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = APOE_genotype)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#4C72B0") +
  labs(
    title = "APOE genotype distribution",
    x = "APOE genotype",
    y = "Number of participants"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  )

# =========================================================
# 12. PLOT ε4 CARRIER STATUS
# =========================================================

BISC %>%
  mutate(
    APOE_e4_carrier = factor(
      APOE_e4_carrier,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c(
        "Non-carrier",
        "ε4 carrier"
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(APOE_e4_carrier)) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = APOE_e4_carrier,
      fill = APOE_e4_carrier
    )
  ) +
  geom_bar() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Non-carrier" = "#2ca02c",
      "ε4 carrier" = "#d62728"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "APOE ε4 carrier status",
    x = "",
    y = "Number of participants"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```

#### Medication grouping {.tabset}

Medication text variables (`qm4_med1_12w_m`, `qm4_med2_12w_m`, `qm4_med3_12w_m`) have been grouped by medication type using AI tools.

```{r medication_table_html, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Combine all three medication columns and get unique entries
med_unique <- BISC %>%
  select(qm4_med1_12w_m, qm4_med2_12w_m, qm4_med3_12w_m) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), values_to = "medication") %>%
  filter(!is.na(medication), medication != "") %>%
  distinct(medication) %>%
  arrange(medication)

# Create new grouped medication variables without modifying originals
BISC <- BISC %>%
  mutate(
    qm4_med1_group = case_when(
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med1_12w_m), "IBUPROFEN|IBUPORFENO|IBUPROFE|DICLOFENACO|ENANTYUM|ESPIDIFEN|DOLOVOLTAREN|DOBUPAL|DOLALGIAL|KETOPROFENO|NAPROXENO|TRAMADOL|METAMIZOL|NOLOTIL") ~ "NSAIDs / Analgesics",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med1_12w_m), "DIAZEPAM|DORMICUM|ESCITALOPRAM|FLUOXETINA|ABILIFY|CITALOPRAM|ANAFRANIL|PAROXETINA|SERTRALINA|TRYPTIZOL|TRIPTISON|TRIPTIFOL|LYRICA|RELAXANTE|RELPAX|RIZATRIPTAN") ~ "Psychotropics / Antidepressants / Anxiolytics",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med1_12w_m), "AC FOLIC|ACID FOLIC|FOLIC|FOLIDOCE|ISOVORIN ACIDO LEVOFOLINICO|B12|CARIBAN|FEMIBION|NATALBEN|VITAMIN|VIT D|VITAMINA|SUPLEMENTOS|SUPRADYN|SOLARAY|OLIOVITA|OMEGA3|MULTICENTRUM|EXTRACTE DE SHITTAKE") ~ "Folic Acid / Vitamins / Supplements",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med1_12w_m), "ACETILCISTEINA|BUDESONIDA|DUO RESP|FLUMIL|SALBUTAMOL|VENTOLIN|SYMBICORT|SIMBICORT|SERETIDE") ~ "Respiratory / Mucolytics",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med1_12w_m), "AMOXICILINA|AUGMENTINE|AZITROMICINA|MINOCIN|MOXIFLOXACINO|ZITROMAX|ANTIBIOTIC|ANTIBIOTICO") ~ "Antibiotics / Antimicrobials",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med1_12w_m), "ESTRADIOL|GONAL|UTROGESTAN|PROGESTERONA|PROGESTE|MENOPUR|ORGALUTRAN|OVITRELLE|OVITREL|NUVARING|HORMONAS|TRATAMIENTO HORMONAL|IBIS|OVUSITOL|PROGYLUTON|PROGINOVA|PROGYNUTON|PARCHES") ~ "Hormones / Fertility",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med1_12w_m), "CETIRIZINA|EBASTEL|LEVOCETIRIZINA|LORATIDINA|ATARAX|POLARAMINE") ~ "Antihistamines / Allergy",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med1_12w_m), "ATORVASTATINA|BISOPROLOL|HEPARINA|ADIRO|RAMIPRIL|VALSARTAN") ~ "Cardiovascular / Anticoagulants",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med1_12w_m), "HIDROCORTISONA|CORTICOIDES|CORTISONA|PREDNISONA|PREDFORTE") ~ "Corticosteroids",
      !is.na(qm4_med1_12w_m) ~ "Other / Miscellaneous"  # only for filled entries not matched
      # NAs stay NA
),
    qm4_med2_group = case_when(
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med2_12w_m), "IBUPROFEN|IBUPORFENO|IBUPROFE|DICLOFENACO|ENANTYUM|ESPIDIFEN|DOLOVOLTAREN|DOBUPAL|DOLALGIAL|KETOPROFENO|NAPROXENO|TRAMADOL|METAMIZOL|NOLOTIL") ~ "NSAIDs / Analgesics",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med2_12w_m), "DIAZEPAM|DORMICUM|ESCITALOPRAM|FLUOXETINA|ABILIFY|CITALOPRAM|ANAFRANIL|PAROXETINA|SERTRALINA|TRYPTIZOL|TRIPTISON|TRIPTIFOL|LYRICA|RELAXANTE|RELPAX|RIZATRIPTAN") ~ "Psychotropics / Antidepressants / Anxiolytics",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med2_12w_m), "AC FOLIC|ACID FOLIC|FOLIC|FOLIDOCE|ISOVORIN ACIDO LEVOFOLINICO|B12|CARIBAN|FEMIBION|NATALBEN|VITAMIN|VIT D|VITAMINA|SUPLEMENTOS|SUPRADYN|SOLARAY|OLIOVITA|OMEGA3|MULTICENTRUM|EXTRACTE DE SHITTAKE") ~ "Folic Acid / Vitamins / Supplements",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med2_12w_m), "ACETILCISTEINA|BUDESONIDA|DUO RESP|FLUMIL|SALBUTAMOL|VENTOLIN|SYMBICORT|SIMBICORT|SERETIDE") ~ "Respiratory / Mucolytics",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med2_12w_m), "AMOXICILINA|AUGMENTINE|AZITROMICINA|MINOCIN|MOXIFLOXACINO|ZITROMAX|ANTIBIOTIC|ANTIBIOTICO") ~ "Antibiotics / Antimicrobials",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med2_12w_m), "ESTRADIOL|GONAL|UTROGESTAN|PROGESTERONA|PROGESTE|MENOPUR|ORGALUTRAN|OVITRELLE|OVITREL|NUVARING|HORMONAS|TRATAMIENTO HORMONAL|IBIS|OVUSITOL|PROGYLUTON|PROGINOVA|PROGYNUTON|PARCHES") ~ "Hormones / Fertility",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med2_12w_m), "CETIRIZINA|EBASTEL|LEVOCETIRIZINA|LORATIDINA|ATARAX|POLARAMINE") ~ "Antihistamines / Allergy",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med2_12w_m), "ATORVASTATINA|BISOPROLOL|HEPARINA|ADIRO|RAMIPRIL|VALSARTAN") ~ "Cardiovascular / Anticoagulants",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med2_12w_m), "HIDROCORTISONA|CORTICOIDES|CORTISONA|PREDNISONA|PREDFORTE") ~ "Corticosteroids",
      !is.na(qm4_med1_12w_m) ~ "Other / Miscellaneous"  # only for filled entries not matched
      # NAs stay NA
),
    qm4_med3_group = case_when(
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med3_12w_m), "IBUPROFEN|IBUPORFENO|IBUPROFE|DICLOFENACO|ENANTYUM|ESPIDIFEN|DOLOVOLTAREN|DOBUPAL|DOLALGIAL|KETOPROFENO|NAPROXENO|TRAMADOL|METAMIZOL|NOLOTIL") ~ "NSAIDs / Analgesics",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med3_12w_m), "DIAZEPAM|DORMICUM|ESCITALOPRAM|FLUOXETINA|ABILIFY|CITALOPRAM|ANAFRANIL|PAROXETINA|SERTRALINA|TRYPTIZOL|TRIPTISON|TRIPTIFOL|LYRICA|RELAXANTE|RELPAX|RIZATRIPTAN") ~ "Psychotropics / Antidepressants / Anxiolytics",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med3_12w_m), "AC FOLIC|ACID FOLIC|FOLIC|FOLIDOCE|ISOVORIN ACIDO LEVOFOLINICO|B12|CARIBAN|FEMIBION|NATALBEN|VITAMIN|VIT D|VITAMINA|SUPLEMENTOS|SUPRADYN|SOLARAY|OLIOVITA|OMEGA3|MULTICENTRUM|EXTRACTE DE SHITTAKE|YODUK") ~ "Folic Acid / Vitamins / Supplements",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med3_12w_m), "ACETILCISTEINA|BUDESONIDA|DUO RESP|FLUMIL|SALBUTAMOL|VENTOLIN|SYMBICORT|SIMBICORT|SERETIDE") ~ "Respiratory / Mucolytics",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med3_12w_m), "AMOXICILINA|AUGMENTINE|AZITROMICINA|MINOCIN|MOXIFLOXACINO|ZITROMAX|ANTIBIOTIC|ANTIBIOTICO") ~ "Antibiotics / Antimicrobials",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med3_12w_m), "ESTRADIOL|GONAL|UTROGESTAN|PROGESTERONA|PROGESTE|MENOPUR|ORGALUTRAN|OVITRELLE|OVITREL|NUVARING|HORMONAS|TRATAMIENTO HORMONAL|IBIS|OVUSITOL|PROGYLUTON|PROGINOVA|PROGYNUTON|PARCHES") ~ "Hormones / Fertility",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med3_12w_m), "CETIRIZINA|EBASTEL|LEVOCETIRIZINA|LORATIDINA|ATARAX|POLARAMINE") ~ "Antihistamines / Allergy",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med3_12w_m), "ATORVASTATINA|BISOPROLOL|HEPARINA|ADIRO|RAMIPRIL|VALSARTAN") ~ "Cardiovascular / Anticoagulants",
      str_detect(toupper(qm4_med3_12w_m), "HIDROCORTISONA|CORTICOIDES|CORTISONA|PREDNISONA|PREDFORTE") ~ "Corticosteroids",
      !is.na(qm4_med1_12w_m) ~ "Other / Miscellaneous"  # only for filled entries not matched
      # NAs stay NA
  )
)

# Summarize per participant
BISC_long <- BISC %>%
  select(id_bisc, qm4_med1_group, qm4_med2_group, qm4_med3_group) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("qm4_med"),
    names_to = "med_number",
    values_to = "med_group"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(med_group))  # remove NAs if participant has <3 meds

# Summarize per participant
# Count per medication group per participant and pivot wider
med_group_counts <- BISC_long %>%
  group_by(id_bisc, med_group) %>%
  summarise(n_meds = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = med_group,
    values_from = n_meds,
    values_fill = 0
  )

# Join at the end of the original dataframe
BISC <- BISC %>%
  left_join(med_group_counts, by = "id_bisc")

############# Generate a summary

# Pivot medications into long format
BISC_long <- BISC %>%
  select(id_bisc, qm4_med1_group, qm4_med2_group, qm4_med3_group) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("qm4_med"),
    names_to = "med_number",
    values_to = "med_group"
  )

# Count how many meds per participant per group
med_counts <- BISC_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(med_group)) %>%  # ignore truly missing meds
  group_by(id_bisc, med_group) %>%
  summarise(n_meds = n(), .groups = "drop")

# Include participants with 0 meds in some groups
all_counts <- med_counts %>%
  complete(
    id_bisc = unique(BISC$id_bisc),
    med_group = unique(BISC_long$med_group),
    fill = list(n_meds = 0)
  )

# Demographic summary: number of participants with 0,1,2,3 meds in each group
medication_table <- all_counts %>%
  group_by(med_group, n_meds) %>%
  summarise(n_participants = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(med_group, n_meds) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = n_meds,
    values_from = n_participants,
    names_prefix = "n_meds_",
    values_fill = 0
  )

# Remove the column for 0 if we only want participants taking at least 1
medication_table <- medication_table %>%
  select(med_group, everything())

medication_table %>%
  kable("html", caption = "Number of unique participants by medication group and number of meds taken of that group") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
```

#### Education re-categorization {.tabset}

Education has been reorganized. Variable `educ_level_m_2cat` will be created from `educ_level_m_3cat`.

```{r education_table_html, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(BISC, aes(x = educ_level_m_3cat)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Education Level (3 categories)",
       x = "Education Level",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

library(dplyr)

BISC <- BISC %>%
  mutate(educ_level_m_2cat = case_when(
    educ_level_m_3cat %in% c("Primary or less", "Secondary") ~ "Up to Secondary",
    educ_level_m_3cat == "University" ~ "University",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))

ggplot(BISC, aes(x = educ_level_m_2cat)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "darkgreen") +
  labs(title = "Education Level (2 categories)",
       x = "Education Level",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

BISC$educ_level_m_2cat <- factor(
  BISC$educ_level_m_2cat,
  levels = c("Up to Secondary", "University")
)
```

#### Ethnics re-categorization {.tabset}

Ethnics has been reorganized. Variable `educ_level_m_2cat` will be created from `educ_level_m_3cat`.

```{r ethnics_table_html, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(dplyr)

BISC <- BISC %>%
  mutate(ethnicity_m_2cat = case_when(
    ethnicity_m_3cat == "eur" ~ "European",
    ethnicity_m_3cat %in% c("lat_amr", "other") ~ "Other",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))

BISC$ethnicity_m_2cat <- factor(
  BISC$ethnicity_m_2cat,
  levels = c("European", "Other")
)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(BISC, aes(x = ethnicity_m_3cat)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "green") +
  labs(title = "Ethnicity (3 categories)",
       x = "Ethnicity",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(BISC, aes(x = ethnicity_m_2cat)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "tomato") +
  labs(title = "Ethnicity (2 categories)",
       x = "Ethnicity",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

#### SCL90r global score {.tabset}

SCL90r total test score has been created (`scl_total_m_20w`, `scl_total_p_20w` and `scl_total_m_y4`). y4 will be calculated if at least 80% of the items are filled.

```{r scl90rtotalscore, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}

########################################### 20w
m_items <- c(
  "T_som_m","T_obs_m","T_si_m","T_dep_m",
  "T_ans_m","T_hos_m","T_fob_m","T_par_m",
  "T_psic_m","T_igs_m","T_tsp_m","T_imsp_m"
)

p_items <- c(
  "T_som_p","T_obs_p","T_si_p","T_dep_p",
  "T_ans_p","T_hos_p","T_fob_p","T_par_p",
  "T_psic_p","T_igs_p","T_tsp_p","T_imsp_p"
)

BISC$scl_total_m_20w <- rowSums(BISC[, m_items], na.rm = TRUE)
BISC$scl_total_p_20w <- rowSums(BISC[, p_items], na.rm = TRUE)

ggplot(BISC, aes(x = scl_total_m_20w)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "red") +
  labs(title = "SCL90r total score (mother, 20w)",
       x = "Risk score",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(BISC, aes(x = scl_total_p_20w)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "orange") +
  labs(title = "SCL90r total score (partner, 20w)",
       x = "Risk score",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

########################################### y4

m_items_y4 <- c(
  "scl90r_T_som_m_48m",
  "scl90r_T_obs_m_48m",
  "scl90r_T_si_m_48m",
  "scl90r_T_dep_m_48m",
  "scl90r_T_ans_m_48m",
  "scl90r_T_hos_m_48m",
  "scl90r_T_fob_m_48m",
  "scl90r_T_par_m_48m",
  "scl90r_T_psic_m_48m",
  "scl90r_T_igs_m_48m",
  "scl90r_T_tsp_m_48m",
  "scl90r_T_imsp_m_48m"
)

BISC[m_items_y4] <- lapply(BISC[m_items_y4], function(x){

  dplyr::case_when(
    x == "At risk" ~ 1,
    x == "Not at risk" ~ 0,
    x %in% c("NA", "", "Missing", "missing") ~ NA_real_,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )
})

################ 80% of filled data at least to be calculated
BISC$scl_total_m_y4 <- ifelse(
  rowSums(!is.na(BISC[m_items_y4])) >= 10,
  rowSums(BISC[m_items_y4], na.rm = TRUE),
  NA
)


ggplot(BISC, aes(x = scl_total_m_y4)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "red", color = "white") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "SCL-90R total score (mother, 4 years)",
    x = "Total score",
    y = "Count"
  )
```




EPDS entries have been re-categorized in new variables (`epds_risk_m_32w_mod`, `epds.risk_m_2m_6c_mod`, `epds.risk_m_12m_6c_mod`, `epds.risk_m_18m_6c_mod`).

```{r epdsnewcategories, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
BISC <- BISC %>%
  mutate(
    # ---- 32w ----
    epds_risk_m_32w_mod = case_when(
      epds_risk_m_32w %in% c("low", "Low") ~ "Low",
      epds_risk_m_32w %in% c("middle", "middle_suic", "Middle", "Middle with suicidality") ~ "Middle",
      epds_risk_m_32w %in% c("high", "high_suic", "High", "High with suicidality") ~ "High",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # ---- 2m ----
    epds.risk_m_2m_6c_mod = case_when(
      epds.risk_m_2m_6c %in% c("low", "Low") ~ "Low",
      epds.risk_m_2m_6c %in% c("middle", "middle_suic", "Middle", "Middle with suicidality") ~ "Middle",
      epds.risk_m_2m_6c %in% c("high", "high_suic", "High", "High with suicidality") ~ "High",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # ---- 12m ----
    epds.risk_m_12m_6c_mod = case_when(
      epds.risk_m_12m_6c %in% c("low", "Low") ~ "Low",
      epds.risk_m_12m_6c %in% c("middle", "middle_suic", "Middle", "Middle with suicidality") ~ "Middle",
      epds.risk_m_12m_6c %in% c("high", "high_suic", "High", "High with suicidality") ~ "High",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # ---- 18m ----
    epds.risk_m_18m_6c_mod = case_when(
      epds.risk_m_18m_6c %in% c("low", "Low") ~ "Low",
      epds.risk_m_18m_6c %in% c("middle", "middle_suic", "Middle", "Middle with suicidality") ~ "Middle",
      epds.risk_m_18m_6c %in% c("high", "high_suic", "High", "High with suicidality") ~ "High",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

BISC <- BISC %>%
  mutate(
    epds_risk_m_32w_mod = factor(epds_risk_m_32w_mod, levels = c("Low","Middle","High")),
    epds.risk_m_2m_6c_mod = factor(epds.risk_m_2m_6c_mod, levels = c("Low","Middle","High")),
    epds.risk_m_12m_6c_mod = factor(epds.risk_m_12m_6c_mod, levels = c("Low","Middle","High")),
    epds.risk_m_18m_6c_mod = factor(epds.risk_m_18m_6c_mod, levels = c("Low","Middle","High"))
  )
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)

# -----------------------------
# Variables
# -----------------------------
epds_vars <- c(
  "epds_risk_m_32w",
  "epds.risk_m_2m_6c",
  "epds.risk_m_12m_6c",
  "epds.risk_m_18m_6c"
)

epds_mod_vars <- c(
  "epds_risk_m_32w_mod",
  "epds.risk_m_2m_6c_mod",
  "epds.risk_m_12m_6c_mod",
  "epds.risk_m_18m_6c_mod"
)

time_labels <- c(
  "32 weeks",
  "2 months",
  "12 months",
  "18 months"
)

# -----------------------------
# Color palettes
# -----------------------------
cols_original <- c(
  "low" = "#2C7BB6",
  "middle" = "#FDAE61",
  "middle_suic" = "#F46D43",
  "high" = "#D73027",
  "high_suic" = "#A50026"
)

cols_mod <- c(
  "Low" = "#2C7BB6",
  "Middle" = "#FDAE61",
  "High" = "#D73027"
)

# -----------------------------
# Theme
# -----------------------------
theme_epds <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
}

# -----------------------------
# Plot function
# -----------------------------
make_epds_plot <- function(var_orig, var_mod, title) {

  df_orig <- BISC %>%
    transmute(value = .data[[var_orig]]) %>%
    filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
    mutate(group = "Original")

  df_mod <- BISC %>%
    transmute(value = .data[[var_mod]]) %>%
    filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
    mutate(group = "Modified")

  df <- bind_rows(df_orig, df_mod) %>%
    mutate(value = tolower(as.character(value)))

  ggplot(df, aes(x = value, fill = value)) +
    geom_bar(color = "black", alpha = 0.85) +
    facet_wrap(~group, nrow = 1) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = NULL,
      y = "N participants"
    ) +
    theme_epds() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(cols_original, cols_mod))
}

plots <- lapply(seq_along(epds_vars), function(i) {

  make_epds_plot(
    epds_vars[i],
    epds_mod_vars[i],
    time_labels[i]
  )

})

plots
```
#### COVID infection binary {.tabset}

```{r covidbinary, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
BISC <- BISC %>%
  mutate(
    
    # -----------------------------
    # COVID 2 months
    # -----------------------------
    covid_m2_mother = case_when(
      is.na(b9_covid.yesno_m_2m_3c) ~ NA_real_,
      grepl("No", b9_covid.yesno_m_2m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 0,
      grepl("Sí", b9_covid.yesno_m_2m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    covid_m2_child = case_when(
      is.na(b13_covid.yesno_c_2m_3c) ~ NA_real_,
      grepl("No", b13_covid.yesno_c_2m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 0,
      grepl("Sí", b13_covid.yesno_c_2m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    # -----------------------------
    # COVID 6 months
    # -----------------------------
    covid_m6_mother = case_when(
      is.na(q22_covid.yesno_m_6m_3c) ~ NA_real_,
      grepl("No", q22_covid.yesno_m_6m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 0,
      grepl("Sí", q22_covid.yesno_m_6m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    covid_m6_child = case_when(
      is.na(q26_covid.yesno_c_6m_3c) ~ NA_real_,
      grepl("No", q26_covid.yesno_c_6m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 0,
      grepl("Sí", q26_covid.yesno_c_6m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    # -----------------------------
    # COVID 12 months
    # -----------------------------
    covid_m12_mother = case_when(
      is.na(q20_covid.yesno_m_12m_3c) ~ NA_real_,
      grepl("No", q20_covid.yesno_m_12m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 0,
      grepl("Sí", q20_covid.yesno_m_12m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    covid_m12_child = case_when(
      is.na(q24_covid.yesno_c_12m_3c) ~ NA_real_,
      grepl("No", q24_covid.yesno_c_12m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 0,
      grepl("Sí", q24_covid.yesno_c_12m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    # -----------------------------
    # COVID 18 months
    # -----------------------------
    covid_m18_mother = case_when(
      is.na(covid1_covid.yesno_m_18m_3c) ~ NA_real_,
      grepl("No", covid1_covid.yesno_m_18m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 0,
      grepl("Sí", covid1_covid.yesno_m_18m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    covid_m18_child = case_when(
      is.na(covid3_covid.yesno_c_18m_3c) ~ NA_real_,
      grepl("No", covid3_covid.yesno_c_18m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 0,
      grepl("Sí", covid3_covid.yesno_c_18m_3c, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )
```

#### Gestational age (4y) in weeks {.tabset}

```{r gestage4y, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

BISC <- BISC %>%
  mutate(
    gestage_4y_weeks = gestage_4y_days / 7
  )

library(ggplot2)

# -------------------------
# 1. Gestational age in DAYS
# -------------------------
p1 <- ggplot(BISC, aes(x = gestage_4y_days)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of gestational age at 4y (days)",
    x = "Days",
    y = "Count"
  )

# -------------------------
# 2. Gestational age in WEEKS
# -------------------------
p2 <- ggplot(BISC, aes(x = gestage_4y_weeks)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "tomato", alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of gestational age at 4y (weeks)",
    x = "Weeks",
    y = "Count"
  )

p1
p2
```

#### Adding variables from other datasets {.tabset}

```{r newvars, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
objs <- load("C:/Users/U127925/Desktop/FLH/Analyses/External/BISC - 05032026/databases/newvariables_260616.RData")


library(dplyr)

############################################################
# 1. Check IDs before merging
############################################################

cat("Rows in BISC:", nrow(BISC), "\n")
cat("Rows in df:", nrow(df), "\n\n")

cat("Unique participants in BISC:",
    n_distinct(BISC$id_bisc), "\n")

cat("Unique participants in df:",
    n_distinct(df$id_bisc), "\n\n")


############################################################
# 2. Check duplicates in df
# (there should be ONE row per participant)
############################################################

dup_df <- df %>%
  count(id_bisc) %>%
  filter(n > 1)

cat("Participants duplicated in df:",
    nrow(dup_df), "\n")

if(nrow(dup_df) > 0){
  print(dup_df)
  stop("df contains duplicated participants.")
}


############################################################
# 3. Variables to import
############################################################

vars_to_add <- setdiff(
  names(df),
  "id_bisc"
)

############################################################
# 4. QC before merge
############################################################

filled_before <- sapply(
  vars_to_add,
  function(v){

    if(v %in% names(BISC)){

      BISC %>%
        distinct(id_bisc, .data[[v]]) %>%
        summarise(
          n = sum(!is.na(.data[[v]]))
        ) %>%
        pull(n)

    } else {

      NA

    }

  }
)

############################################################
# 5. Merge
############################################################

BISC_before <- nrow(BISC)

BISC <- BISC %>%
  left_join(
    df,
    by = "id_bisc",
    suffix = c("", "_new")
  )

############################################################
# 6. QC after merge
############################################################

cat("\n")
cat("Rows before merge :", BISC_before, "\n")
cat("Rows after merge  :", nrow(BISC), "\n\n")

cat("Unique participants after merge:",
    n_distinct(BISC$id_bisc), "\n\n")

############################################################
# 7. Number of filled participants
############################################################

filled_after <- sapply(
  vars_to_add,
  function(v){

    BISC %>%
      distinct(id_bisc, .data[[v]]) %>%
      summarise(
        n = sum(!is.na(.data[[v]]))
      ) %>%
      pull(n)

  }
)

qc <- data.frame(

  Variable = vars_to_add,

  Filled_before = filled_before,

  Filled_after = filled_after,

  Added = filled_after - filled_before

)

print(qc)

############################################################
# 8. Verify values are identical across visits
############################################################

consistency <- sapply(
  vars_to_add,
  function(v){
    BISC %>%
      group_by(id_bisc) %>%
      summarise(
        n_values = n_distinct(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      filter(n_values > 1) %>%
      nrow()
  }
)
cat("\nParticipants with inconsistent values across visits:\n")
print(
  data.frame(
    Variable = vars_to_add,
    Inconsistent_participants = consistency
  )
)

############################################################
# 9. Final summary
############################################################

cat("\n--------------------------------------\n")
cat("Merge completed\n")
cat("--------------------------------------\n")
cat("Rows unchanged:",
    nrow(BISC) == BISC_before,
    "\n")

cat("Participants unchanged:",
    n_distinct(BISC$id_bisc),
    "\n")

cat("Variables added:",
    length(vars_to_add),
    "\n")
```



## 1. Descriptive tables {.tabset}

### 1.0. General demographics {.tabset}



### 1.1. Mother demographics {.tabset}

```{r demographics11, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(dplyr)
library(forcats)
library(gtsummary)
library(tidyr)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Define variable groups
# -----------------------------
demographics_vars <- c(
  "age_12w_m",
  "bmi_m_12w",
  "educ_level_m_2cat",
  "ethnicity_m_2cat",
  "hosp_recruit_m_12w",
  "parity_m_2cat",
  "parity_m",
  "APOE_genotype",
  "APOE_e4_carrier",
    "ses_gini",
  "qs24_finance_12w_m",
  "gq_econ_cur.level_6cat_m_48m"
)
comorbidities_vars <- c(
  "qm23_high_bp_12w_m","qm23_dyslip_12w_m","qm23_diab1_12w_m","qm23_diab2_12w_m",
  "qm23_isch_12w_m","qm23_struc_12w_m","qm23_stroke_12w_m",
  "qm23_kidney_12w_m","qm23_coag_12w_m","qm23_thromb_12w_m",
  "qm23_urin_12w_m","qm23_thyroid_12w_m","qm23_autoimm_12w_m",
  "qm23_asthma_12w_m","qm23_rhini_12w_m","qm23_derma_12w_m",
  "qm23_migr_12w_m","qm23_epil_12w_m","qm23_tumour_12w_m",
  "qm23_psych_12w_m","qm23_digest_12w_m","qm23_others_12w_m"
)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Recode demographics & comorbidities
# -----------------------------
BISC <- BISC %>%
    mutate(
    across(
      all_of(comorbidities_vars),
      ~ factor(
          na_if(as.character(.x), "I don't know"),
          levels = c("Yes", "No")
        )
    ),
    hosp_recruit_m_12w = fct_infreq(factor(
      case_when(
        substr(id_bisc, 1, 2) == "10" ~ "SP: Hospital de Sant Pau",
        substr(id_bisc, 1, 2) == "11" ~ "MT: Hospital Clínic Maternitat",
        substr(id_bisc, 1, 2) == "12" ~ "SJD: Hospital Sant Joan de Déu",
        TRUE ~ "Other"
      ),
      levels = c("SP: Hospital de Sant Pau","MT: Hospital Clínic Maternitat","SJD: Hospital Sant Joan de Déu","Other")
    ))
  )
BISC <- BISC %>%
  mutate(
    parity_m_2cat = factor(parity_m_2cat),
    parity_m = as.numeric(parity_m)
  )

BISC <- BISC %>%
  mutate(

    qs24_finance_12w_m = factor(
      qs24_finance_12w_m,
      levels = c(
        "We live comfortably",
        "We are fine",
        "We are as good as we can",
        "It turns out to be quite difficult",
        "It is very difficult",
        "I would rather not answer"
      )
    ),

    gq_econ_cur.level_6cat_m_48m = factor(
      gq_econ_cur.level_6cat_m_48m,
      levels = c(
        "living_comfortably",
        "doing_well",
        "getting_by",
        "quite_difficult",
        "very_difficult",
        "prefer_not_to_answer"
      ),
      labels = c(
        "Living comfortably",
        "Doing well",
        "Getting by",
        "Quite difficult",
        "Very difficult",
        "Prefer not to answer"
      )
    )

  )

BISC <- BISC %>%
  mutate(
    # ensure clean factor for genotype table
    APOE_genotype = factor(APOE_genotype),

    # convert carrier to labelled factor for table
    APOE_e4_carrier = factor(
      APOE_e4_carrier,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Non-carrier", "ε4 carrier")
    )
  )

# -----------------------------
# 3. Labels
# -----------------------------
labels_demo <- list(
  age_12w_m = "Age",
  bmi_m_12w = "BMI",
  educ_level_m_2cat = "Education",
  ethnicity_m_2cat = "Ethnicity",
  hosp_recruit_m_12w = "Hospital of recruitment",
  parity_m_2cat = "Parity (nulliparous / multiparous)",
  parity_m = "Number of previous births (>22 weeks)",
  APOE_genotype = "APOE genotype",
  APOE_e4_carrier = "APOE ε4 carrier",
  ses_gini = "Neighborhood socioeconomic status (SES index)",
  qs24_finance_12w_m = "Perceived household financial situation (12w)",
  gq_econ_cur.level_6cat_m_48m = "Current household economic situation (48m)"
)

labels_comorb_total <- list(
  qm23_high_bp_12w_m = "Arterial hypertension",
  qm23_dyslip_12w_m = "Dyslipemia",
  qm23_diab1_12w_m = "Diabetes type 1",
  qm23_diab2_12w_m = "Diabetes type 2",
  qm23_isch_12w_m = "Ischemic disease",
  qm23_struc_12w_m = "Structural heart disease",
  qm23_stroke_12w_m = "Stroke",
  qm23_kidney_12w_m = "Chronic kidney disease",
  qm23_coag_12w_m = "Clotting disorders",
  qm23_thromb_12w_m = "History of thrombosis",
  qm23_urin_12w_m = "Urinary tract pathology",
  qm23_thyroid_12w_m = "Thyroid disorders",
  qm23_autoimm_12w_m = "Autoimmune diseases",
  qm23_asthma_12w_m = "Asthma",
  qm23_rhini_12w_m = "Allergic rhinitis",
  qm23_derma_12w_m = "Atopic dermatitis",
  qm23_migr_12w_m = "Migraine",
  qm23_epil_12w_m = "Epilepsy",
  qm23_tumour_12w_m = "Tumors",
  qm23_psych_12w_m = "Psychiatric pathology",
  qm23_digest_12w_m = "Digestive problems",
  qm23_others_12w_m = "Other comorbidities"
)

# -----------------------------
# 4. Create tables separately
# -----------------------------
tbl_demo <- tbl_summary(
  data = BISC,
  include = demographics_vars,
  label = labels_demo,
  statistic = list(
    all_continuous() ~ "{median} ({p25}, {p75})",
    all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"
  ),
  missing = "no"
) %>%
  add_n() %>%
  modify_header(stat_0 = "**Participants**") %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  modify_caption("Table: Mother characteristics (12 weeks)")

tbl_comorb <- tbl_summary(
  data = BISC,
  include = comorbidities_vars,
  label = labels_comorb_total,
  statistic = all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)",
  missing = "no"
) %>%
  add_n() %>%
  modify_header(stat_0 = "**Participants**") %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  modify_caption("Table: Mother Comorbidities (pre-pregnancy)")

# -----------------------------
# 5. Medication table preparation
# -----------------------------
med_flags <- BISC_long %>%
  mutate(taken = 1) %>%
  select(id_bisc, med_group, taken) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = med_group, values_from = taken, values_fill = 0) %>%
  mutate(across(-id_bisc, ~factor(., levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No", "Yes"))))

# Optional reorder
med_order <- c(
  "NSAIDs / Analgesics",
  "Psychotropics / Antidepressants / Anxiolytics",
  "Folic Acid / Vitamins / Supplements",
  "Respiratory / Mucolytics",
  "Antibiotics / Antimicrobials",
  "Hormones / Fertility",
  "Antihistamines / Allergy",
  "Cardiovascular / Anticoagulants",
  "Corticosteroids",
  "Other / Miscellaneous"
)
med_flags <- med_flags %>% select(id_bisc, any_of(med_order))

tbl_medications <- tbl_summary(
  data = med_flags,
  include = -id_bisc,
  statistic = all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)",
  missing = "no"
) %>%
  add_n() %>%
  modify_header(stat_0 = "**Participants**") %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  modify_caption("Table: Participants taking at least one medication per group (12 weeks)")


## Mother Demographics
tbl_demo
```

### 1.2. Comorbidities (pre-pregnancy) {.tabset}

```{r comorbidities, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
tbl_comorb
```
