<span style=font-weight:bold;">
BIOMARKERS DURING PREGNANCY: BY GA
</span>


```{r ga plotall3 v13, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
long_v13 <- long_df %>%
  filter(Visit %in% c("v1", "v2", "v3")) %>%
  mutate(Visit = factor(Visit, levels = c("v1", "v2", "v3")))
plot_spaghetti(long_v13, "log_pTau217")
plot_spaghetti(long_v13, "log_NFL")
plot_spaghetti(long_v13, "log_GFAP")
```


#### Functional form of biomarker–GA {.tabset}

<span style="color:#2E8B57; font-weight:bold;">
Does the biomarker change linearly with gestational age across individuals?
</span>

_______________________________________________________________________________________________________

**a) AIC**: Lower AIC indicates better model fit. However, with only four timepoints, cubic terms can easily overfit the data and capture noise rather than true developmental structure, so further analyses are needed.

_______________________________________________________________________________________________________

**b) Bootstrap**: Resampling helps showing if the patterns found are consistent. "Sd" shows how much the estimated shape changes when the sample changes slightly. 

- Mean proximal to zero -> shape not supported for the data

- Low sd -> shape is reliable among our population

- High sd -> the shape is not robust

_______________________________________________________________________________________________________

**c) Spline analysis**: To evaluate whether polynomial assumptions were appropriate.

- 1 dominant spline component -> Suggests an approximately linear or gently monotonic relationship.

- 2 dominant spline components -> Suggests a single meaningful curvature (e.g., U-shape or inverted U-shape), consistent with a quadratic-like pattern.

- 3 dominant spline components -> Suggests a more complex trajectory with multiple inflection points within the observed range. This indicates that a simple linear model is insufficient, although with limited timepoints this may also reflect over-flexibility.

_______________________________________________________________________________________________________

**d) Predicted distribution plots**: For each biomarker, predicted trajectories are generated from linear, quadratic, and cubic mixed models across the observed range of gestational age. These plots can be used to (1) visually compare functional forms, (2) assess whether curvature is smooth or driven by extreme values, (3) evaluate consistency with the raw data (“spaghetti plots”), (4) detect overfitting (e.g., unrealistic oscillations in cubic models)

_______________________________________________________________________________________________________

##### Exploring all visits (standard)

```{r quadratic3, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(lme4)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(splines)
library(broom.mixed)
library(lmerTest)

# -----------------------
# PREP
# -----------------------
data_prep <- function(df){

  df %>%
    mutate(
      id_bisc = factor(id_bisc)
    )
}

# -----------------------
# AIC MODELS
# -----------------------
run_aic_models <- function(data, outcome) {

  f1 <- as.formula(paste0(outcome, " ~ GA_weeks + (1 | id_bisc)"))
  f2 <- as.formula(paste0(outcome, " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + (1 | id_bisc)"))
  f3 <- as.formula(paste0(outcome, " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + (1 | id_bisc)"))

  m1 <- lmer(f1, data = data, REML = FALSE)
  m2 <- lmer(f2, data = data, REML = FALSE)
  m3 <- lmer(f3, data = data, REML = FALSE)

  tibble(
    Model = c("m1", "m2", "m3"),
    Formula = c(
      paste0(outcome, " ~ GA_weeks + (1 | id_bisc)"),
      paste0(outcome, " ~ GA_weeks + GA_weeks^2 + (1 | id_bisc)"),
      paste0(outcome, " ~ GA_weeks + GA_weeks^2 + GA_weeks^3 + (1 | id_bisc)")
    ),
    df = c(attr(logLik(m1), "df"),
           attr(logLik(m2), "df"),
           attr(logLik(m3), "df")),
    AIC = c(AIC(m1), AIC(m2), AIC(m3))
  )
}

# -----------------------
# BOOTSTRAP
# -----------------------
bootstrap_ga <- function(data, outcome, B = 200){

  ids <- unique(data$id_bisc)
  coefs <- matrix(NA, B, 3)

  for (b in 1:B){

    boot_ids <- sample(ids, replace = TRUE)

    boot_data <- data %>%
      filter(id_bisc %in% boot_ids)

    m <- lm(
      as.formula(paste0(
        outcome,
        " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3)"
      )),
      data = boot_data
    )

    coefs[b, ] <- coef(m)[c("GA_weeks",
                            "I(GA_weeks^2)",
                            "I(GA_weeks^3)")]
  }

  colnames(coefs) <- c("Linear", "Quadratic", "Cubic")

  data.frame(
    Term = colnames(coefs),
    Mean = colMeans(coefs, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD   = apply(coefs, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# -----------------------
# SPLINE MODEL
# -----------------------
fit_spline <- function(data, outcome){

  lmer(
    as.formula(paste0(
      outcome,
      " ~ ns(GA_weeks, df = 3) + (1|id_bisc)"
    )),
    data = data,
    REML = FALSE
  )
}

# -----------------------
# SPLINE INTERPRETATION (NEW ✔)
# -----------------------
spline_table <- function(model){

  td <- broom.mixed::tidy(model, effects = "fixed") %>%
    filter(grepl("ns\\(GA_weeks", term)) %>%
    mutate(
      significance = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
        p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ "ns"
      )
    )

  return(td)
}

spline_interpretation <- function(spline_tab){

  sig <- sum(spline_tab$significance != "ns")

  if (sig == 0){
    return("Linear / monotonic pattern (no spline components significant)")
  }

  if (sig == 1){
    return("Mostly linear or gently monotonic trend")
  }

  if (sig == 2){
    return("One meaningful curvature (quadratic-like pattern)")
  }

  if (sig == 3){
    return("Complex trajectory or possible overfitting (given limited timepoints)")
  }
}

# -----------------------
# PREDICTIVE PLOTS
# -----------------------
plot_ga_models <- function(data, outcome){

  grid <- data.frame(
    GA_weeks = seq(min(data$GA_weeks), max(data$GA_weeks), length.out = 200)
  )

  m_lin <- lmer(as.formula(paste0(outcome, " ~ GA_weeks + (1|id_bisc)")),
                data = data, REML = FALSE)

  m_quad <- lmer(as.formula(paste0(outcome,
                                   " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + (1|id_bisc)")),
                 data = data, REML = FALSE)

  m_cub <- lmer(as.formula(paste0(outcome,
                                  " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + (1|id_bisc)")),
                data = data, REML = FALSE)

  grid$linear <- predict(m_lin, newdata = grid, re.form = NA)
  grid$quadratic <- predict(m_quad, newdata = grid, re.form = NA)
  grid$cubic <- predict(m_cub, newdata = grid, re.form = NA)

  grid_long <- pivot_longer(
    grid,
    cols = c(linear, quadratic, cubic),
    names_to = "model",
    values_to = "pred"
  )

  ggplot(grid_long, aes(GA_weeks, pred, color = model)) +
    geom_line(size = 1.2) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = paste(outcome, "GA functional form comparison"),
      x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
      y = outcome
    )
}

plot_ga_raw <- function(data, outcome){

  ggplot(data, aes(x = GA_weeks, y = .data[[outcome]])) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "black") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = paste(outcome, "Raw data distribution with LOESS trend"),
      x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
      y = outcome
    )
}
# -----------------------
# FULL PIPELINE (UPDATED ✔)
# -----------------------
run_ga_pipeline <- function(df, outcome){

  df2 <- data_prep(df)

  # ---- AIC ----
  aic <- run_aic_models(df2, outcome)

  print(
    kable(aic, format = "html", digits = 2,
          caption = paste0(outcome, ": AIC comparison of gestational age models")) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  # ---- BOOTSTRAP ----
  boot <- bootstrap_ga(df2, outcome, B = 200)

  print(
    kable(boot, format = "html", digits = 6,
          caption = paste0(outcome, ": bootstrap coefficient stability")) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  # ---- SPLINE (NEW STRUCTURE ✔) ----
  spline_model <- fit_spline(df2, outcome)

  spline_tab <- spline_table(spline_model)

  print(
    kable(spline_tab, format = "html", digits = 3,
          caption = paste0(outcome, ": spline component significance")) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  # interpretation line as table (safe for knitting)
  interp <- data.frame(
    Interpretation = spline_interpretation(spline_tab)
  )

  print(
    kable(interp, format = "html",
          caption = paste0(outcome, ": spline interpretation")) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  # ---- PLOT ----
  print(plot_ga_models(df2, outcome))
  
    # ---- RAW DISTRIBUTION PLOT ----
  print(plot_ga_raw(df2, outcome))
}


# -----------------------
# RUN MODELS
# -----------------------
run_ga_pipeline(long_df, "log_pTau217")
run_ga_pipeline(long_df, "log_NFL")
run_ga_pipeline(long_df, "log_GFAP")
```

_______________________________________________________________________________________________________


**CONCLUSION (v1-v4)**

**pTau217** --> Probably **Quadratic model**

 `log_pTau217 ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + (1 | id_bisc)`

**NFL** --> Probably **Cubic model**

 `log_NFL ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + (1 | id_bisc)`

**GFAP** --> Probably **Cubic model**

 `log_GFAP ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + (1 | id_bisc)`

 
##### Exploring all visits (piecewise)

```{r piecewise, results='hide', message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
long_df <- long_df %>%
  mutate(
    post_birth = ifelse(Visit == "v4", 1, 0)
  )
long_df <- long_df %>%
  mutate(
    GA_pre  = ifelse(post_birth == 0, GA_weeks, max(GA_weeks[Visit != "v4"], na.rm=TRUE)),
    
    GA_post = ifelse(post_birth == 1,
                     GA_weeks - max(GA_weeks[Visit != "v4"], na.rm=TRUE),
                     0)
  )
m_piece <- lmer(
  log_GFAP ~ GA_pre + GA_post + (1 | id_bisc),
  data = long_df
)
m_piece



library(splines)

m_spline <- lmer(
  log_GFAP ~ ns(GA_weeks, knots = 40) + (1|id_bisc),
  data = long_df
)
m_spline
```
```{r testspliness, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
########################### SELECT THE BEST KNOT
compare_knots <- function(df, biomarker, knots = c(34, 36, 38, 40, 42, 44)) {

  library(lme4)
  library(splines)
  library(dplyr)
  library(knitr)
  library(kableExtra)

  df <- df %>%
    mutate(id_bisc = factor(id_bisc))

  # -----------------------------
  # FIT MODELS
  # -----------------------------
  models <- lapply(knots, function(k) {

    fml <- as.formula(
      paste0(
        biomarker,
        " ~ ns(GA_weeks, knots = ", k, ") + (1 | id_bisc)"
      )
    )

    lmer(fml, data = df, REML = FALSE)
  })

  # -----------------------------
  # TABLE
  # -----------------------------
  tab <- data.frame(
    knot = knots,
    AIC = sapply(models, AIC),
    BIC = sapply(models, BIC),
    logLik = sapply(models, logLik)
  ) %>%
    arrange(AIC)

  # -----------------------------
  # HTML OUTPUT
  # -----------------------------
  cat("Biomarker:", biomarker, "\n\n")

  print(
    tab %>%
      mutate(logLik = round(as.numeric(logLik), 2)) %>%
      kable(
        format = "html",
        digits = 2,
        caption = paste0(
          biomarker,
          ": spline knot comparison (GA functional form)"
        )
      ) %>%
      kable_styling(
        full_width = FALSE,
        bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive")
      ) %>%
      row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)
  )

  invisible(list(models = models, table = tab))
}
compare_knots(long_df, "log_pTau217")
compare_knots(long_df, "log_NFL")
compare_knots(long_df, "log_GFAP")
```

They are similar, let's use 40 weeks as a knot, since it's the most normal value for birth and biologically meaningful.

```{r testspliness PTAU217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
################################################## COMPARE MODELS
compare_ga_models <- function(df, biomarker, knot = 40){

  library(lme4)
  library(lmerTest)
  library(splines)
  library(dplyr)
  library(knitr)
  library(kableExtra)

  # -----------------------------
  # PREP
  # -----------------------------
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(id_bisc = factor(id_bisc))

  # -----------------------------
  # MODELS
  # -----------------------------
  f_linear <- as.formula(paste0(biomarker, " ~ GA_weeks + (1 | id_bisc)"))
  f_quadratic <- as.formula(paste0(biomarker, " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + (1 | id_bisc)"))
  f_cubic <- as.formula(paste0(biomarker, " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + (1 | id_bisc)"))
  f_spline <- as.formula(paste0(biomarker, " ~ ns(GA_weeks, knots = ", knot, ") + (1 | id_bisc)"))

  m_linear <- lmer(f_linear, data = df, REML = FALSE)
  m_quadratic <- lmer(f_quadratic, data = df, REML = FALSE)
  m_cubic <- lmer(f_cubic, data = df, REML = FALSE)
  m_spline <- lmer(f_spline, data = df, REML = FALSE)

  # -----------------------------
  # TABLE
  # -----------------------------
  tab <- tibble(
    Model = c("Linear","Quadratic","Cubic", paste0("Spline (knot = ", knot, ")")),

    Formula = c(
      paste(deparse(f_linear), collapse = " "),
      paste(deparse(f_quadratic), collapse = " "),
      paste(deparse(f_cubic), collapse = " "),
      paste(deparse(f_spline), collapse = " ")
    ),

    AIC = c(AIC(m_linear), AIC(m_quadratic), AIC(m_cubic), AIC(m_spline)),
    BIC = c(BIC(m_linear), BIC(m_quadratic), BIC(m_cubic), BIC(m_spline)),
    logLik = c(logLik(m_linear), logLik(m_quadratic), logLik(m_cubic), logLik(m_spline))
  ) %>%
    arrange(AIC)

  # -----------------------------
  # OUTPUT (ONLY ONCE)
  # -----------------------------
  cat("Biomarker:", biomarker, "\n\n")

  print(
    tab %>%
      mutate(logLik = round(as.numeric(logLik), 2)) %>%
      kable(
        format = "html",
        digits = 2,
        caption = paste0(
          biomarker,
          ": comparison of gestational age functional forms"
        )
      ) %>%
      kable_styling(
        full_width = FALSE,
        bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive")
      ) %>%
      row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)
  )

  invisible(list(
    linear = m_linear,
    quadratic = m_quadratic,
    cubic = m_cubic,
    spline = m_spline,
    table = tab
  ))
}
compare_ga_models(long_df, "log_pTau217", knot = 40)
```

```{r testspliness NFL, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
compare_ga_models(long_df, "log_NFL", knot = 40)
```

```{r testspliness GFAP, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
compare_ga_models(long_df, "log_GFAP", knot = 40)
```

**CONCLUSION (v1-v4, piecewise model)**

**pTau217** --> Probably **Cubic model**

 `log_pTau217 ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + (1 | id_bisc)`

**NFL** --> Probably **Cubic model**

 `log_NFL ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + (1 | id_bisc)`

**GFAP** --> Probably **Cubic model**

 `log_GFAP ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + (1 | id_bisc)`


##### Exploring pregnancy only

```{r pregnancy only polynom, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_ga_pipeline(long_v13, "log_pTau217")
run_ga_pipeline(long_v13, "log_NFL")
run_ga_pipeline(long_v13, "log_GFAP")
```

#### Analyses {.tabset}

Pregnancy with blood acquired before (PRE) or after (POST) delivery at v3

##### General {.tabset}

<span style="color:#2E8B57; font-weight:bold; font-size:1.05em;">
Model 1: Do biomarker levels differ across pregnancy and postpartum visits? 
</span>

<span style="font-weight:bold;">
Longitudinal - pregnancy and post-partum: (v1-v4) 
</span>

- `Biomarker ~ Visit + (1 | id_bisc)`

<span style="color:#2E8B57; font-weight:bold;">
Model 2: Do biomarker levels differ during pregnancy?
</span>

<span style="font-weight:bold;">
Longitudinal - pregnancy: (v1-v3) 
</span>

- `Biomarker ~ GA_weeks + (1 | id_bisc)`

<span style="color:#2E8B57; font-weight:bold;">
Model 3: Do biomarker levels differ during pregnancy, accounting for covariates?
</span>

<span style="font-weight:bold;">
Longitudinal - pregnancy: (v1-v3) 
</span>

- `Biomarker ~ GA_weeks + covariates + (1 | id_bisc)`


```{r ga ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.show='hold'}
######################################################################################################################################################################
############################################################# GENERAL ANALYSIS #######################################################################################
######################################################################################################################################################################
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(broom.mixed)
library(kableExtra)
library(emmeans)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

run_biomarker_time <- function(df, bio){

  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      Visit = factor(Visit, levels = c("v1","v2","v3","v4")),
      id_bisc = factor(id_bisc)
    )
  # Biomarker-specific color
bio_col <- bio_colors[[bio]]

# Lighter version for fills
bio_fill <- scales::alpha(bio_col, 0.25)

# Fallback if biomarker is not in bio_colors
if (is.null(bio_col)) {
  bio_col  <- "#2C7FB8"
  bio_fill <- scales::alpha(bio_col, 0.25)
}

  # -----------------------------
  # MODEL 1: VISIT MODEL
  # -----------------------------
  m_visit <- lmer(
    as.formula(paste0(bio, " ~ Visit + (1 | id_bisc)")),
    data = df,
    REML = TRUE
  )

  em_visit <- emmeans(m_visit, ~ Visit)

  visit_pairs <- pairs(em_visit) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    mutate(
      p_fmt = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "<0.001",
                     sprintf("%.3f", p.value)),
      signif = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
        p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    )

  # =========================================================
  # MODEL 2a: PREGNANCY ONLY (v1-v3)
  # =========================================================

df_preg <- df %>%
  filter(Visit %in% c("v1","v2","v3"))

if (bio == "log_pTau217") {

  df_preg <- df_preg %>%
    mutate(
      storage_time = case_when(
        Visit == "v1" ~ as.numeric(bl_edta.time.col.stor_m_12w),
        Visit == "v2" ~ as.numeric(bl_edta.time.col.stor_m_32w),
        Visit == "v3" ~ as.numeric(bl_time.col.store.edta_m_0y)
      )
    )

} else {

  df_preg <- df_preg %>%
    mutate(
      storage_time = case_when(
        Visit == "v1" ~ as.numeric(bl_plas.time.col.stor_m_12w),
        Visit == "v2" ~ as.numeric(bl_plas.time.col.stor_m_32w),
        Visit == "v3" ~ as.numeric(bl_time.col.store.plas_m_0y)
      )
    )
}

  if (bio == "log_NFL") {
    fml_preg <- as.formula(
      paste0(bio, " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + (1 | id_bisc)")
    )
  } else {
    fml_preg <- as.formula(
      paste0(bio, " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + (1 | id_bisc)")
    )
  }

  m_preg <- lmer(fml_preg, data = df_preg, REML = TRUE)

  # =========================================================
  # MODEL 2b: PREGNANCY + POSTPARTUM (v1 - v4)
  # HIDDEN / DISABLED
  # =========================================================

  # if (bio == "log_pTau217") {
  #   fml_ga <- as.formula(
  #     paste0(bio, " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + (1 | id_bisc)")
  #   )
  # } else {
  #   fml_ga <- as.formula(
  #     paste0(bio, " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + (1 | id_bisc)")
  #   )
  # }

  # m_ga <- lmer(fml_ga, data = df, REML = TRUE)

  #########################################################
  # MODEL 3: Adjusted pregnancy model
  #########################################################

if (bio == "log_NFL") {

  fml_preg_adj <- as.formula(
    paste0(
      bio,
      " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + ",
      "hosp_recruit_m_12w + ",
      "age_12w_m + ",
#      "storage_time + ",
      "(1|id_bisc)"
    )
  )
} else {
  fml_preg_adj <- as.formula(
    paste0(
      bio,
      " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + ",
      "hosp_recruit_m_12w + ",
      "age_12w_m + ",
#      "storage_time + ",
      "(1|id_bisc)"
    )
  )
}

m_preg_adj <- lmer(
  fml_preg_adj,
  data = df_preg,
  REML = TRUE
)

  # =========================================================
  # HEADER LABELS
  # =========================================================

  preg_model_label <- if (bio == "log_NFL") {
    "-> Cubic"
  } else {
    "-> Quadratic"
  }
  cat("\n\n")
  cat("**Type**: Longitudinal mixed model\n\n")
  cat("**Dataset**: Complete dataset\n\n")

  # =========================================================
  # MODEL 1 FORMULA
  # =========================================================

  cat("**Model 1: Visit model (v1-v4)**\n")

  cat("```r\n")
  cat(deparse(formula(m_visit)))
  cat("\n```\n\n")

  # =========================================================
  # MODEL 2 and 3 FORMULA
  # =========================================================

  cat("**Model 2: Pregnancy GA model (v1–v3)**\n")
  cat(paste0("**", preg_model_label, "**\n\n"))

  cat("```r\n")
  cat(deparse(fml_preg))
  cat("\n```\n\n")

  cat("**Model 3: Adjusted pregnancy GA model (v1–v3)**\n")

  cat(paste0("**", preg_model_label, "**\n\n"))

  cat("```r\n")
  cat(deparse(fml_preg_adj))
  cat("\n```\n\n")
  
  cat("**──────────────────────────────────────────────**\n\n")
  
  # =========================================================
  # MODEL 1 TABLE
  # =========================================================

  tab_visit <- broom.mixed::tidy(m_visit, effects = "fixed") %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      estimate = round(estimate, 3),
      std.error = round(std.error, 3),
      p_fmt = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "<0.001",
                     sprintf("%.3f", p.value)),
      signif = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
        p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    )

  cat("**Model 1: Visit model - coefficients**\n\n")

  print(
    tab_visit %>%
      select(term, estimate, std.error, p_fmt, signif) %>%
      kable(format = "html",
            col.names = c("Term","Beta","SE","p","Sig.")) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  # =========================================================
  # MODEL 1 PAIRWISE
  # =========================================================

  cat("\n\n**Model 1: Visit model - Pairwise comparisons**\n\n")

  print(
    visit_pairs %>%
      select(contrast, estimate, SE, p_fmt, signif) %>%
      kable(format = "html",
            col.names = c("Comparison","Diff","SE","p","Sig.")) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  # =========================================================
  # MODEL 2 and 3 TABLE
  # =========================================================

  tab_preg <- broom.mixed::tidy(m_preg, effects = "fixed") %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      estimate = round(estimate, 3),
      std.error = round(std.error, 3),
      p_fmt = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "<0.001",
                     sprintf("%.3f", p.value)),
      signif = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
        p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    )

  cat("\n\n**Model 2: Pregnancy GA model (v1–v3) - coefficients**\n\n")

  print(
    tab_preg %>%
      select(term, estimate, std.error, p_fmt, signif) %>%
      kable(format = "html",
            col.names = c("Term","Beta","SE","p","Sig.")) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )
  
  
  tab_preg_adj <- broom.mixed::tidy(
  m_preg_adj,
  effects = "fixed"
) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    estimate = round(estimate,3),
    std.error = round(std.error,3),
    p_fmt = ifelse(
      p.value < 0.001,
      "<0.001",
      sprintf("%.3f", p.value)
    ),
    signif = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )
  )

cat("\n\n**Model 3: Adjusted pregnancy model (v1–v3) - coefficients**\n\n")

print(
  tab_preg_adj %>%
    select(term, estimate, std.error, p_fmt, signif) %>%
    kable(
      format = "html",
      col.names = c("Term","Beta","SE","p","Sig.")
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
)
# =========================================================
# SAMPLE SIZE
# =========================================================

cat("\n\n**Sample size**\n\n")

sample_tab <- data.frame(
  Model = c(
    "Model 1: Visit",
    "Model 2: Pregnancy GA",
    "Model 3: Adjusted pregnancy"
  ),
  N = c(
    nobs(m_visit),
    nobs(m_preg),
    nobs(m_preg_adj)
  )
)

print(
  sample_tab %>%
    kable(format = "html") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
)
# =========================================================
# DATA AVAILABILITY
# =========================================================

cat("\n\n**Availability of variables used in the models**\n\n")

vars_to_check <- c(
  bio,
  "Visit",
  "GA_weeks",
  "id_bisc",
  "age_12w_m",
  "hosp_recruit_m_12w"
 # "storage_time"
)

availability_tab <- df_preg %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      all_of(vars_to_check),
      ~sum(!is.na(.))
    )
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to = "Variable",
    values_to = "Available_N"
  )

print(
  availability_tab %>%
    kable(format = "html",
          col.names = c("Variable", "Available N")) %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
)
  
  # =========================================================
  # RETURN
  # =========================================================

  invisible(list(
  m_visit = m_visit,
  m_preg = m_preg,
  m_preg_adj = m_preg_adj
))
}
  
############################################################
## PLOTS FOR BIOMARKER (NOT SEPARATING PRE-POST)
############################################################

plot_biomarker_time <- function(df, biomarker){

  ##########################################################
  ## Prepare data
  ##########################################################

  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      Visit = factor(
        Visit,
        levels = c("v1","v2","v3","v4")
      )
    )

  ## Biomarker colour
  bio_col <- bio_colors[[biomarker]]

  ## Fallback
  if(is.null(bio_col)){
    bio_col <- "#2C7FB8"
  }

  ## Transparent fill
  bio_fill <- alpha(bio_col, 0.25)

  ##########################################################
  ## Pregnancy only
  ##########################################################

  preg <- df %>%
    filter(Visit %in% c("v1","v2","v3"))

  ##########################################################
  ## 1. Boxplots by visit
  ##########################################################

  p_box <-

    ggplot(
      df,
      aes(
        x = Visit,
        y = .data[[biomarker]]
      )
    ) +

    geom_boxplot(
      fill = bio_fill,
      colour = bio_col,
      alpha = 0.8,
      outlier.shape = NA
    ) +

    geom_jitter(
      colour = bio_col,
      alpha = 0.35,
      width = 0.12,
      size = 1.5
    ) +

    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +

    labs(
      title = "Biomarker distribution across visits",
      x = "Visit",
      y = biomarker
    ) +

    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  print(p_box)

  ##########################################################
  ## 2. Pregnancy spaghetti plot
  ##########################################################

  p_spag_preg <-
    ggplot(
      preg,
      aes(
        x = GA_weeks,
        y = .data[[biomarker]],
        group = id_bisc
      )
    ) +
    geom_line(
      colour = "grey70",
      alpha = .30
    ) +
    geom_point(
      colour = bio_col,
      alpha = .50,
      size = 1.3
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    labs(
      title = "Pregnancy spaghetti plot",
      x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
      y = biomarker
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
  print(p_spag_preg)

  ##########################################################
  ## 3. Pregnancy trajectory
  ##########################################################

  p_traj_preg <-
    ggplot(
      preg,
      aes(
        x = GA_weeks,
        y = .data[[biomarker]]
      )
    ) +
    geom_smooth(
      method = "loess",
      se = TRUE,
      colour = bio_col,
      fill = bio_fill,
      linewidth = 1.5
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    labs(
      title = "Pregnancy trajectory",
      x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
      y = biomarker
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
  print(p_traj_preg)

  ##########################################################
  ## 4. Whole-study spaghetti plot
  ##########################################################

  p_spag_all <-
    ggplot(
      df,
      aes(
        x = GA_weeks,
        y = .data[[biomarker]],
        group = id_bisc
      )
    ) +
    geom_line(
      colour = "grey70",
      alpha = .30
    ) +
    geom_point(
      colour = bio_col,
      alpha = .50,
      size = 1.3
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    labs(
      title = "Whole-study spaghetti plot",
      x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
      y = biomarker
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
  print(p_spag_all)

  ##########################################################
  ## 5. Whole-study trajectory
  ##########################################################

  p_traj_all <-

    ggplot(
      df,
      aes(
        x = GA_weeks,
        y = .data[[biomarker]]
      )
    ) +

    geom_smooth(
      method = "loess",
      se = TRUE,
      colour = bio_col,
      fill = bio_fill,
      linewidth = 1.5
    ) +

    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +

    labs(
      title = "Whole-study trajectory",
      x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
      y = biomarker
    ) +

    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  print(p_traj_all)

}
######################################################################################################################################################################
############################################################# stratifying on blood sample before/after birth #########################################################
######################################################################################################################################################################
run_biomarker_time_by_delivery <- function(df, bio){

  library(dplyr)
  library(lme4)
  library(lmerTest)
  library(broom.mixed)
  library(kableExtra)
  library(emmeans)

  ############################################################
  # Prepare data
  ############################################################

  df <- df %>%
    filter(
      bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y %in% c(0,1)
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      DeliveryGroup = factor(
        bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y,
        levels=c(0,1),
        labels=c("PRE","POST")
      ),
      Visit=factor(
        Visit,
        levels=c("v1","v2","v3","v4")
      ),
      id_bisc=factor(id_bisc)
    )

  ############################################################
  # Loop over PRE / POST
  ############################################################

  for(gr in levels(df$DeliveryGroup)){
    cat("<hr style='border:2px solid #2C3E50;'>")
cat(
  paste0(
    "<div style='font-size:20px;font-weight:bold;margin-top:10px;'>",
    bio,
    " — ",
    gr,
    "</div><br>"
  )
)

    dat <- df %>%

      filter(
        DeliveryGroup==gr
      )

    ##########################################################
    # N
    ##########################################################

    cat(
      paste0(
        "<b>Participants:</b> ",
        n_distinct(dat$id_bisc),
        "<br>"
      )
    )
    cat(
      paste0(
        "<b>Observations:</b> ",
        nrow(dat),
        "<br><br>"
      )
    )

    ##########################################################
    # MODEL 1
    ##########################################################

    m_visit <-
      lmer(
        as.formula(
          paste0(
            bio,
            " ~ Visit + (1|id_bisc)"
          )
        ),
        data=dat,
        REML=TRUE
      )
    ##########################################################
    # Pairwise
    ##########################################################
    visit_pairs <-
      pairs(
        emmeans(
          m_visit,
          ~Visit
        )
      ) %>%
      as.data.frame() %>%
      mutate(
        p_fmt=ifelse(
          p.value<0.001,
          "<0.001",
          sprintf("%.3f",p.value)
        ),
        signif=case_when(
          p.value<0.001~"***",
          p.value<0.01~"**",
          p.value<0.05~"*",
          TRUE~""
        )
      )
    ##########################################################
    # MODEL 2
    ##########################################################
    dat_preg <-
      dat %>%
      filter(
        Visit %in% c("v1","v2","v3")
      )

if(bio == "log_pTau217"){

  dat_preg <- dat_preg %>%
    mutate(
      storage_time = case_when(
        Visit == "v1" ~ as.numeric(bl_edta.time.col.stor_m_12w),
        Visit == "v2" ~ as.numeric(bl_edta.time.col.stor_m_32w),
        Visit == "v3" ~ as.numeric(bl_time.col.store.edta_m_0y)
      )
    )

}else{

  dat_preg <- dat_preg %>%
    mutate(
      storage_time = case_when(
        Visit == "v1" ~ as.numeric(bl_plas.time.col.stor_m_12w),
        Visit == "v2" ~ as.numeric(bl_plas.time.col.stor_m_32w),
        Visit == "v3" ~ as.numeric(bl_time.col.store.plas_m_0y)
      )
    )

}
    if(bio=="log_NFL"){
      fml <-
        as.formula(
          paste0(
            bio,
            " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3) + (1|id_bisc)"
          )
        )
    }else{
      fml <-
        as.formula(
          paste0(
            bio,
            " ~ GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + (1|id_bisc)"
          )
        )
    }
    m_preg <-
      lmer(
        fml,
        data=dat_preg,
        REML=TRUE
      )

##########################################################
# MODEL 3 (Adjusted)
##########################################################
if(bio == "log_NFL"){
  fml_adj <-
    as.formula(
      paste0(
        bio,
        " ~ ",
        "GA_weeks + ",
        "I(GA_weeks^2) + ",
        "I(GA_weeks^3) + ",
        "hosp_recruit_m_12w + ",
        "age_12w_m + ",
#        "storage_time + ",
        "(1|id_bisc)"
      )
    )
}else{
  fml_adj <-
    as.formula(
      paste0(
        bio,
        " ~ ",
        "GA_weeks + ",
        "I(GA_weeks^2) + ",
        "hosp_recruit_m_12w + ",
        "age_12w_m + ",
#        "storage_time + ",
        "(1|id_bisc)"
      )
    )
}
m_adj <-
  lmer(
    fml_adj,
    data = dat_preg,
    REML = TRUE
  )

    ##########################################################
    # Visit coefficients
    ##########################################################

    cat("<div style='font-size:16px;font-weight:bold;margin-top:15px;'>Model 1: Visit model</div><br>")
    tab1 <-
      broom.mixed::tidy(
        m_visit,
        effects="fixed"
      ) %>%
      filter(
        term!="(Intercept)"
      ) %>%
      mutate(
        estimate=round(estimate,3),
        std.error=round(std.error,3),
        p_fmt=ifelse(
          p.value<0.001,
          "<0.001",
          sprintf("%.3f",p.value)
        ),
        signif=case_when(
          p.value<0.001~"***",
          p.value<0.01~"**",
          p.value<0.05~"*",
          TRUE~""
        )
      )
    print(
      tab1 %>%
        select(
          term,
          estimate,
          std.error,
          p_fmt,
          signif
        ) %>%

        kbl(
          format="html",
          col.names=c(
            "Term",
            "Beta",
            "SE",
            "p",
            "Sig."
          )
        ) %>%

        kable_styling(
          full_width=FALSE,
          bootstrap_options=c(
            "striped",
            "hover",
            "condensed"
          )
        )
    )

    ##########################################################
    # Pairwise
    ##########################################################
    cat("<div style='font-size:16px;font-weight:bold;margin-top:15px;'>Model 1: Visit pairwise comparisons</div><br>")
    print(
      visit_pairs %>%
        select(
          contrast,
          estimate,
          SE,
          p_fmt,
          signif
        ) %>%

        kbl(
          format="html",
          col.names=c(
            "Comparison",
            "Difference",
            "SE",
            "p",
            "Sig."
          )
        ) %>%

        kable_styling(
          full_width=FALSE,
          bootstrap_options=c(
            "striped",
            "hover",
            "condensed"
          )
        )
    )

    ##########################################################
    # Pregnancy coefficients
    ##########################################################
    cat("<div style='font-size:16px;font-weight:bold;margin-top:15px;'>Model 2: Pregnancy GA model</div><br>")
    tab2 <-
      broom.mixed::tidy(
        m_preg,
        effects="fixed"
      ) %>%
      filter(
        term!="(Intercept)"
      ) %>%
      mutate(
        estimate=round(estimate,3),
        std.error=round(std.error,3),
        p_fmt=ifelse(
          p.value<0.001,
          "<0.001",
          sprintf("%.3f",p.value)
        ),
        signif=case_when(
          p.value<0.001~"***",
          p.value<0.01~"**",
          p.value<0.05~"*",
          TRUE~""
        )
      )
    print(
      tab2 %>%
        select(
          term,
          estimate,
          std.error,
          p_fmt,
          signif
        ) %>%

        kbl(
          format="html",
          col.names=c(
            "Term",
            "Beta",
            "SE",
            "p",
            "Sig."
          )
        ) %>%

        kable_styling(
          full_width=FALSE,
          bootstrap_options=c(
            "striped",
            "hover",
            "condensed"
          )
        )
      )
##########################################################
# Model 3 coefficients
##########################################################

cat("<div style='font-size:16px;font-weight:bold;margin-top:15px;'>Model 3: Pregnancy GA model (adjusted)</div><br>")
tab3 <-
  broom.mixed::tidy(
    m_adj,
    effects = "fixed"
  ) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    estimate = round(estimate,3),
    std.error = round(std.error,3),
    p_fmt = ifelse(
      p.value < 0.001,
      "<0.001",
      sprintf("%.3f",p.value)
    ),
    signif = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )
  )

print(
  tab3 %>%
    select(
      term,
      estimate,
      std.error,
      p_fmt,
      signif
    ) %>%
    kbl(
      format = "html",
      col.names = c(
        "Term",
        "Beta",
        "SE",
        "p",
        "Sig."
      )
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      full_width = FALSE,
      bootstrap_options = c(
        "striped",
        "hover",
        "condensed"
      )
    )
)
  }
}

############################################################
## PLOTS: PRE vs POST DELIVERY GROUPS
############################################################

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

plot_delivery_groups <- function(df, biomarker){

  ##########################################################
  ## Prepare data
  ##########################################################

  df <- df %>%
    filter(
      bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y %in% c(0,1)
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      DeliveryGroup = factor(
        bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y,
        levels=c(0,1),
        labels=c("PRE","POST")
      ),
      Visit=factor(
        Visit,
        levels=c("v1","v2","v3","v4")
      )
    )

  ##########################################################
  ## 1. BOXPLOTS
  ##########################################################

  p_box <-
    ggplot(
      df,
      aes(
        x=DeliveryGroup,
        y=.data[[biomarker]],
        fill=DeliveryGroup
      )
    )+
    geom_boxplot(
      alpha=.70,
      outlier.shape=NA
    )+
    geom_jitter(
      width=.12,
      alpha=.45,
      size=1.8
    )+
    facet_wrap(
      ~Visit,
      nrow=1
    )+
    scale_fill_manual(
      values=c(
        "#702963",
        "#D70040"
      )
    )+
    theme_minimal(base_size=13)+
    labs(
      title=paste0(
        biomarker,
        " by visit"
      ),
      x="",
      y=biomarker
    )+
    theme(
      legend.position="none",
      strip.text=element_text(face="bold"),
      plot.title=element_text(face="bold")
    )
  print(p_box)
  ##########################################################
  ## 2. PREGNANCY SPAGHETTI (v1-v3)
  ##########################################################

  df_preg <-
    df %>%
    filter(
      Visit %in% c("v1","v2","v3")
    )
  p_spag_preg <-
  ggplot(
    df_preg,
    aes(
      x = GA_weeks,
      y = .data[[biomarker]],
      group = id_bisc,
      colour = DeliveryGroup
    )
  ) +
  geom_line(
    alpha = .20,
    linewidth = .5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    alpha = .45,
    size = 1
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      "#702963",
      "#D70040"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    title = "Pregnancy trajectories",
    x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
    y = biomarker,
    colour = "Delivery"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
print(p_spag_preg)
p_trend_preg <-

  ggplot(
    df_preg,
    aes(
      x = GA_weeks,
      y = .data[[biomarker]],
      colour = DeliveryGroup
    )
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    se = TRUE,
    linewidth = 1.5
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      "#702963",
      "#D70040"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    title = "Pregnancy mean trajectory",
    x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
    y = biomarker,
    colour = "Delivery"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
print(p_trend_preg)

  ##########################################################
  ## 3. WHOLE STUDY SPAGHETTI (v1-v4)
  ##########################################################
  p_spag <-
    ggplot(
      df,
      aes(
        x = GA_weeks,
        y = .data[[biomarker]],
        group = id_bisc,
        colour = DeliveryGroup
      )
    ) +
    geom_line(
      alpha = .20,
      linewidth = .5
    ) +
    geom_point(
      alpha = .45,
      size = 1
    ) +
    scale_colour_manual(
      values = c(
        "#702963",
        "#D70040"
      )
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    labs(
      title = "Whole study trajectories (v1–v4)",
      x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
      y = biomarker,
      colour = "Delivery"
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
  print(p_spag)
  
##########################################################
## Whole-study mean trajectory
##########################################################

p_trend_all <-
  ggplot(
    df,
    aes(
      x = GA_weeks,
      y = .data[[biomarker]],
      colour = DeliveryGroup
    )
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    se = TRUE,
    linewidth = 1.5
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      "#702963",
      "#D70040"
    )
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +

  labs(
    title = "Whole-study mean trajectory",
    x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
    y = biomarker,
    colour = "Delivery"
  ) +

  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

print(p_trend_all)
}
```

##### (Storage time analysis) {.tabset}


```{r TEST ANALYSIS STORAGE TIME INFLUENCE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.show='hold'}
# LET'S REPEAT MODEL 3 WITH THE 315 PARTICIPANTS WITH STORAGE TIME AVAILABLE BUT WITHOUT STORAGE TIME AS COVARIATE

get_storage_subset <- function(df, bio) {

  df_preg <- df %>%
    filter(Visit %in% c("v1", "v2", "v3"))

  if (bio == "log_pTau217") {

    df_preg <- df_preg %>%
      mutate(
        storage_time = case_when(
          Visit == "v1" ~ as.numeric(bl_edta.time.col.stor_m_12w),
          Visit == "v2" ~ as.numeric(bl_edta.time.col.stor_m_32w),
          Visit == "v3" ~ as.numeric(bl_time.col.store.edta_m_0y)
        )
      )

  } else {

    df_preg <- df_preg %>%
      mutate(
        storage_time = case_when(
          Visit == "v1" ~ as.numeric(bl_plas.time.col.stor_m_12w),
          Visit == "v2" ~ as.numeric(bl_plas.time.col.stor_m_32w),
          Visit == "v3" ~ as.numeric(bl_time.col.store.plas_m_0y)
        )
      )

  }

  df_preg_storage <- df_preg %>%
    filter(!is.na(storage_time))

  cat("Participants with storage time available:",
      dplyr::n_distinct(df_preg_storage$id_bisc), "\n")

  cat("Observations with storage time available:",
      nrow(df_preg_storage), "\n")

  return(df_preg_storage)
}

run_model3_storage <- function(df, bio){

  library(dplyr)
  library(lme4)
  library(lmerTest)
  library(broom.mixed)
  library(kableExtra)

  # Pregnancy visits only
  df_preg <- df %>%
    filter(Visit %in% c("v1","v2","v3")) %>%
    mutate(
      id_bisc = factor(id_bisc)
    )

  # Create storage_time
  if (bio == "log_pTau217") {

    df_preg <- df_preg %>%
      mutate(
        storage_time = case_when(
          Visit == "v1" ~ as.numeric(bl_edta.time.col.stor_m_12w),
          Visit == "v2" ~ as.numeric(bl_edta.time.col.stor_m_32w),
          Visit == "v3" ~ as.numeric(bl_time.col.store.edta_m_0y)
        )
      )

  } else {

    df_preg <- df_preg %>%
      mutate(
        storage_time = case_when(
          Visit == "v1" ~ as.numeric(bl_plas.time.col.stor_m_12w),
          Visit == "v2" ~ as.numeric(bl_plas.time.col.stor_m_32w),
          Visit == "v3" ~ as.numeric(bl_time.col.store.plas_m_0y)
        )
      )

  }

  # Keep only observations with storage time available
  df_preg <- df_preg %>%
    filter(!is.na(storage_time))

  cat("Participants:", n_distinct(df_preg$id_bisc), "\n")
  cat("Observations:", nrow(df_preg), "\n\n")

  # Model
  if (bio == "log_NFL") {

    fml <- as.formula(
      paste0(
        bio,
        " ~ GA_days + I(GA_days^2) + I(GA_days^3) + ",
 #       "hosp_recruit_m_12w + ",
        "educ_level_m_2cat + ",
        "ethnicity_m_2cat + ",
        "age_12w_m + ",
        "(1 | id_bisc)"
      )
    )

  } else {

    fml <- as.formula(
      paste0(
        bio,
        " ~ GA_days + I(GA_days^2) + ",
 #       "hosp_recruit_m_12w + ",
        "educ_level_m_2cat + ",
        "ethnicity_m_2cat + ",
        "age_12w_m + ",
        "(1 | id_bisc)"
      )
    )

  }

  model <- lmer(
    fml,
    data = df_preg,
    REML = TRUE
  )

  tab <- broom.mixed::tidy(model, effects = "fixed") %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      estimate = round(estimate, 3),
      std.error = round(std.error, 3),
      p = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", p.value)),
      signif = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
        p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    )

  print(
    tab %>%
      select(term, estimate, std.error, p, signif) %>%
      kable(
        format = "html",
        col.names = c("Term", "Beta", "SE", "p", "Sig.")
      ) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  invisible(model)
}

# run_model3_storage(long_df, "log_pTau217")
run_model3_storage(long_df, "log_NFL")
# run_model3_storage(long_df, "log_GFAP")

##################################################################################################################
##################################################################################################################
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Gestational age calculated as in the collaborator's script
# GA = collection date - last menstrual period (LMP/FUR)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------

long_df <- long_df %>%
  mutate(

    collection_date = case_when(
      Visit == "v1" ~ as.Date(bl_date.extr_m_12w),
      Visit == "v2" ~ as.Date(bl_date.extr_m_32w),
      Visit == "v3" ~ as.Date(bl_col.date_m_0y),
      Visit == "v4" ~ as.Date(extraction_date)
    ),

    GA_their = as.numeric(
      collection_date -
        as.Date(eco_fur_12w_m)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-collection_date)
```

##### PRE/POST groups {.tabset}

<span style="color:#2E8B57; font-weight:bold;">
Model 1: Does the timing of blood collection relative to delivery (before vs after birth) influence overall biomarker concentrations after accounting for gestational age? (longitudinal)
</span>

<span style="font-weight:bold;">
Longitudinal - pregnancy: (v1-v3) 
</span>

- `Biomarker ~ Blood/Delivery group + GA_weeks + (1|id)`
 

<span style="color:#2E8B57; font-weight:bold;">
Model 2: Do longitudinal biomarker trajectories across gestation differ according to whether the sample was collected before or after delivery? (interaction)
</span>

<span style="font-weight:bold;">
Longitudinal - pregnancy: (v1-v3) 
</span>

- `Biomarker ~ Blood/Delivery group * GA_weeks + GA_weeks + (1|id)`


```{r sensitivity prepostna, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
####################################################
# PACKAGES
####################################################

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(broom)
library(broom.mixed)
library(kableExtra)

####################################################
# DATA PREPARATION
####################################################
long_v13 <- long_v13 %>%
  mutate(
    delivery_group = case_when(
      bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y == 0 ~ "PRE",
      bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y == 1 ~ "POST",
      TRUE ~ "NA"
    ),

    delivery_group = factor(
      delivery_group,
      levels=c("PRE","POST","NA")
    )
  )


#table(long_v13$delivery_group)
analysis_df <- long_v13 %>%
  filter(
    delivery_group %in% c("PRE","POST")
  ) %>%
  droplevels()
```

###### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r ptau217 prepost2, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}

####################################################
# pTau217 (GA quadratic)
####################################################
m_level_PTAU217 <- lmer(
  log_pTau217 ~
    delivery_group +
    I(GA_weeks^2) +
    GA_weeks +
    (1 | id_bisc),
  data = analysis_df
)

cat("
<div style='font-size:18px;font-weight:bold;margin-top:20px'>
Model 1: pTau217 level
</div>

<p>
<b>Formula:</b>
log_pTau217 ~ delivery_group + GA_weeks + GA_weeks^2
</p>
")

broom::tidy(m_level_PTAU217) %>%
kable(
  format="html",
  digits=3
) %>%
kable_styling()

####################################################
# pTau217 trajectory
####################################################

m_pTau_traj <- lmer(
log_pTau217 ~
  GA_weeks *
  delivery_group +
  I(GA_weeks^2) +
  (1|id_bisc),
data=analysis_df
)

cat("
<div style='font-size:18px;font-weight:bold;margin-top:20px'>
Trajectory model 2: pTau217
</div>

<p>
<b>Formula:</b>
log_pTau217 ~ GA_weeks*delivery_group + GA_weeks^2 + (1|id)
</p>
")

broom.mixed::tidy(
  m_pTau_traj
) %>%
kable(
format="html",
digits=3
) %>%
kable_styling()


ggplot(
analysis_df,
aes(
x=GA_weeks,
y=log_pTau217,
color=delivery_group
)
)+

geom_point(
alpha=.4
)+

geom_smooth(
method="lm",
formula=y ~ poly(x,2),
se=TRUE
)+

theme_minimal()+

labs(
title="pTau217 developmental trajectory",
x="Gestational age (weeks)",
y="log pTau217",
color="Delivery group"
)

```

### 2.2. Determinants {.tabset}

<span style="color:#2E8B57; font-weight:bold;">
1) Are the determinants associated with the biomarkers at each visit?
</span>

<span style="font-weight:bold;">
Cross-sectional: (v1,v2,v3,v4) 
</span>

- `Biomarker ~ Determinant + GA_weeks + (1 | id_bisc)`
 

<span style="color:#2E8B57; font-weight:bold;">
2) Are the determinants associated with the trajectory of the biomarkers during prengnacy? Do determinants modify biomarker trajectories during pregnancy?
</span>

<span style="font-weight:bold;">
Longitudinal - pregnancy: (v1-v3)
</span>

- `Biomarker ~ Determinant + GA_weeks + (GA quadratic/cubic component) + (1 | id_bisc)`

- `Biomarker ~ Determinant * (GA_weeks + (GA quadratic/cubic component)) + (1 | id_bisc)`


<span style="color:#2E8B57; font-weight:bold;">
3) Are the determinants associated with the trajectory of the biomarkers during prengnacy and post-partum?
</span>

<span style="font-weight:bold;">
Longitudinal - pregnancy and post-partum: (v1-v4)
</span>

- `Biomarker ~ Determinant + Visit + (1 | id_bisc)`

<br>

_______________________________________________________________________

```{r formula, results='hide', message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# let's create a function to perform the proposed models independently of the biomarker and variables
run_biomarker_variable <- function(df, variable, biomarker){

  library(dplyr)
  library(lme4)
  library(lmerTest)
  library(broom.mixed)
  library(kableExtra)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scales)
  library(tibble)
  library(see)

  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      id_bisc = factor(id_bisc),
      Visit = factor(Visit, levels = c("v1","v2","v3","v4"))
    )

  # =========================================================
  # 0.1) GLOBAL FACTOR + SETUP
  # =========================================================

  is_cat <- is.factor(df[[variable]]) || is.character(df[[variable]])

  if (is_cat) {

    df[[variable]] <- factor(df[[variable]])
    all_levels <- levels(df[[variable]])

    palette_vals <- setNames(
      hue_pal()(length(all_levels)),
      all_levels
    )
  }

  ga_term <- if (biomarker == "log_NFL") {
    "GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3)" # NFL will be modeled as cubic GA; the rest, quadratic
  } else {
    "GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2)"
  }

  # =========================================================
  # 0.2) MODEL OUTPUT
  # =========================================================

  tidy_model <- function(model){

  tab <- broom.mixed::tidy(model, effects = "fixed") %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)")

  if ("effect" %in% colnames(tab)) {
    tab <- tab %>% select(-effect)
  }

  tab %>%
    mutate(
      estimate = round(estimate, 3),
      std.error = round(std.error, 3),

      p_fmt = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
        TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value)
      ),

      signif = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
        p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    )
}

  # =========================================================
  # 0.3) GET N USED IN MODEL
  # =========================================================

  get_model_n <- function(model){

    mf <- model.frame(model)

    tibble(
      n = nrow(mf),
      n_id = if ("id_bisc" %in% names(mf)) {
        dplyr::n_distinct(mf$id_bisc)
      } else {
        NA_integer_
      }
    )
  }

  # =========================================================
  # 0.4) SAFE MODEL WRAPPER
  # =========================================================

  safe_model <- function(formula_txt, data, random = FALSE){

    fml <- as.formula(formula_txt)

    if (random) {

      if (dplyr::n_distinct(data$id_bisc) < nrow(data)) {

        lmer(fml, data = data, REML = TRUE)

      } else {

        lm(
          as.formula(
            gsub("\\+ \\(1 \\| id_bisc\\)", "", formula_txt)
          ),
          data = data
        )
      }

    } else {

      lm(fml, data = data)
    }
  }

  visits <- c("v1","v2","v3","v4")

  # =========================================================
  # 1) CROSS-SECTIONAL MODELS
  # =========================================================

  cat("\n\n<div style='margin:20px 0;'><hr></div>\n\n")
  
  cross_results <- list()
  
  for (v in visits) {

    df_v <- df %>%
      filter(Visit == v)

    if (is_cat) {
      df_v[[variable]] <- factor(
        df_v[[variable]],
        levels = all_levels
      )
    }

    formula_cross <- paste0(
      biomarker,
      " ~ ",
      variable,
      " + GA_weeks + (1 | id_bisc)"
    )

    model_cross <- safe_model(
      formula_cross,
      df_v,
      random = TRUE
    )

    n_info <- get_model_n(model_cross)

    tab <- tidy_model(model_cross) %>%
      mutate(
        Model = paste0("Cross ", v),
        n = n_info$n,
        n_id = n_info$n_id
      )

    cross_results[[v]] <- tab %>%
    mutate(
      Visit = v,
      determinant = variable,
      biomarker = biomarker
    )
      
    cat("\n\n**Visit:", v, "**\n\n")
    cat("```r\n", formula_cross, "\n```\n\n")

    print(
      tab %>%
        select(
          Model,
          n,
          n_id,
          term,
          estimate,
          std.error,
          p_fmt,
          signif
        ) %>%
        kable(format = "html") %>%
        kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
    )

    # =====================================================
    # 1.1) CROSS-SECTIONAL PLOT
    # =====================================================

    if (is_cat) {

      p <- ggplot(
        df_v,
        aes(
          x = .data[[variable]],
          y = .data[[biomarker]],
          fill = .data[[variable]]
        )
      ) +
        geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.5) +
        geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.3) +
        scale_fill_manual(
          values = palette_vals,
          drop = FALSE
        ) +
        theme_minimal() +
        labs(
          x = variable,
          y = biomarker
        ) +
        theme(
          legend.position = "none"
        )

    } else {

      p <- ggplot(
        df_v,
        aes(
          x = .data[[variable]],
          y = .data[[biomarker]]
        )
      ) +
        geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
        theme_minimal() +
        labs(
          x = variable,
          y = biomarker
        )
    }

    print(p)
  }

  # =========================================================
  # 2) PREGNANCY MODEL
  # =========================================================

cat(
  "\n\n<div style='margin:30px 0;'><hr style='border:2px solid #999'></div>\n\n"
)

cat("**📊 LONGITUDINAL: PREGNANCY MODEL (v1–v3)**\n\n")

df_preg <- df %>%
  filter(Visit %in% c("v1","v2","v3"))

if (is_cat) {

  df_preg[[variable]] <- factor(
    df_preg[[variable]],
    levels = all_levels
  )
}

# =========================================================
# 2.1) MAIN EFFECT MODEL
# =========================================================

formula_preg <- paste0(
  biomarker,
  " ~ ",
  variable,
  " + ",
  ga_term,
  " + (1 | id_bisc)"
)

m_preg <- safe_model(
  formula_preg,
  df_preg,
  random = TRUE
)

n_info <- get_model_n(m_preg)

tab_preg <- tidy_model(m_preg) %>%
  mutate(
    Model = "Pregnancy",
    n = n_info$n,
    n_id = n_info$n_id,
    determinant = variable,
    biomarker = biomarker
  )

cat("\n\n```r\n", formula_preg, "\n```\n\n")

print(
  tab_preg %>%
    select(
      Model,
      n,
      n_id,
      term,
      estimate,
      std.error,
      p_fmt,
      signif
    ) %>%
    kable(format = "html") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
)

# =========================================================
# 2.2) INTERACTION MODEL
# =========================================================

cat("\n\n**📈 LONGITUDINAL: PREGNANCY MODEL (v1–v3) - INTERACTION WITH GA_weeks**\n\n")

formula_preg_int <- paste0(
  biomarker,
  " ~ ",
  variable,
  " * (GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2)",
  if (biomarker == "log_NFL") " + I(GA_weeks^3)" else "",
  ") + (1 | id_bisc)"
)

m_preg_int <- safe_model(
  formula_preg_int,
  df_preg,
  random = TRUE
)

tab_preg_int <- tidy_model(m_preg_int) %>%
  mutate(
    Model = "Pregnancy Interaction",
    n = n_info$n,
    n_id = n_info$n_id,
    determinant = variable,
    biomarker = biomarker
  )

cat("\n\n```r\n", formula_preg_int, "\n```\n\n")

print(
  tab_preg_int %>%
    select(
      Model,
      n,
      n_id,
      term,
      estimate,
      std.error,
      p_fmt,
      signif
    ) %>%
    kable(format = "html") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
)
  # =====================================================
  # 2.3) SPAGHETTI PLOT
  # =====================================================

  p1 <- ggplot(
    df_preg,
    aes(
      GA_weeks,
      .data[[biomarker]],
      group = id_bisc
    )
  ) +
    geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
    theme_minimal()

  if (is_cat) {
    p1 <- p1 + aes(color = .data[[variable]])
  }

  print(p1)

  # =====================================================
  # 2.4) SMOOTH PLOT
  # =====================================================

  p2 <- ggplot(
    df_preg,
    aes(
      GA_weeks,
      .data[[biomarker]]
    )
  ) +
    geom_smooth(
      method = "lm",
      se = TRUE,
      linewidth = 1.2
    ) +
    theme_minimal()

  if (is_cat) {
    p2 <- p2 + aes(color = .data[[variable]])
  }

  print(p2)

  # =========================================================
  # 3) POSTPARTUM MODEL
  # =========================================================

  cat(
    "\n\n<div style='margin:30px 0;'><hr style='border:2px solid #999'></div>\n\n"
  )

  cat("**📊 LONGITUDINAL: POSTPARTUM MODEL (v1–v4)**\n\n")

  df_post <- df

  if (is_cat) {

    df_post[[variable]] <- factor(
      df_post[[variable]],
      levels = all_levels
    )
  }

  formula_post <- paste0(
    biomarker,
    " ~ ",
    variable,
    " + Visit + (1 | id_bisc)"
  )

  m_post <- safe_model(
    formula_post,
    df_post,
    random = TRUE
  )

  n_info <- get_model_n(m_post)

  tab_post <- tidy_model(m_post) %>%
    mutate(
      Model = "Postpartum",
      n = n_info$n,
      n_id = n_info$n_id
    )

  cat("\n\n```r\n", formula_post, "\n```\n\n")

  print(
    tab_post %>%
      select(
        Model,
        n,
        n_id,
        term,
        estimate,
        std.error,
        p_fmt,
        signif
      ) %>%
      kable(format = "html") %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  # =====================================================
  # 3.1) POSTPARTUM SPAGHETTI PLOT
  # =====================================================

  p3 <- ggplot(
    df_post,
    aes(
      GA_weeks,
      .data[[biomarker]],
      group = id_bisc
    )
  ) +
    geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
    theme_minimal()

  if (is_cat) {
    p3 <- p3 + aes(color = .data[[variable]])
  }

  print(p3)

  # =====================================================
  # 3.2) POSTPARTUM SMOOTH PLOT
  # =====================================================

  p4 <- ggplot(
    df_post,
    aes(
      GA_weeks,
      .data[[biomarker]]
    )
  ) +
    geom_smooth(
      method = "lm",
      se = TRUE,
      linewidth = 1.2
    ) +
    theme_minimal()

  if (is_cat) {
    p4 <- p4 + aes(color = .data[[variable]])
  }

  print(p4)

  invisible(
  list(
    pregnancy = tab_preg,
    pregnancy_int = tab_preg_int,
    postpartum = tab_post,
    cross = cross_results
  )
)
}

labelled_cols

#  overwrite = TRUE

results <- list()
```




#### Hospital of recruitment {.tabset}

**Result**: No general significant differences between biomarker levels across hospital of recruitment. Found a trend: higher GFAP between SJD - StPau (v1, v2 and longitudinal).

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r hospital ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
#run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "hosp_recruit_m_12w", "log_pTau217")

results[["hosp_pTau"]] <-
  run_biomarker_variable(
    long_df,
    "hosp_recruit_m_12w",
    "log_pTau217"
  )
```



##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r hospital nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}

results[["hosp_NFL"]] <-
  run_biomarker_variable(
    long_df,
    "hosp_recruit_m_12w",
    "log_NFL"
  )
```



##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r hospital gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}

results[["hosp_GFAP"]] <-
  run_biomarker_variable(
    long_df,
    "hosp_recruit_m_12w",
    "log_GFAP"
  )
```

#### APOE {.tabset}

#### Storage time {.tabset}

**Result**: 

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r storage 217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
# to avoid the vnv error i will create a sub-dataframe
storage_df <- biomarkers_time_df %>%
  rename(
    id_bisc = Participant_ID
  ) %>%
  group_by(id_bisc, Visit) %>%
  summarise(                             ################ we should check if we should compute a different storage time per biomarker (?)
    ProcessingTime = first(na.omit(ProcessingTime)),
    TimeGroup      = first(na.omit(TimeGroup)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

TEST_STORAGE <- long_df %>% # not using long_df to avoid the VnV error 
  left_join(
    storage_df,
    by = c(
      "id_bisc",
      "Visit" 
    )
  )

results[["storage_pTau"]] <-
  run_biomarker_variable(
    TEST_STORAGE,
    "TimeGroup",
    "log_pTau217"
  )
```

##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r storagetime nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
#run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "TimeGroup", "log_NFL")

results[["storage_NFL"]] <-
  run_biomarker_variable(
    TEST_STORAGE,
    "TimeGroup",
    "log_NFL"
  )
```

##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r delivery gfap4, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
#run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "TimeGroup", "log_GFAP")

results[["storage_GFAP"]] <-
  run_biomarker_variable(
    TEST_STORAGE,
    "TimeGroup",
    "log_GFAP"
  )
```



#### PMA {.tabset}

**Result**: 

##### pTau217 {.tabset}
